item.title: Middle East | China says it is carrying out “active mediation”
item.description: The Chinese Government stated yesterday that it is developing “active mediation” in the conflict in the Middle East and that it will continue to maintain contacts with the parties involved to promote the return to negotiations. Foreign Ministry spokesman Guo Jiakun said at a press conference that China is “deeply concerned” about tensions and called on all parties to cease military operations to avoid further escalation of the conflict. Guo recalled that the head of Chinese diplomacy, Wang Yi, had recent contacts with several counterparts in the region to “exchange in-depth opinions on the regional situation”. The spokesperson added that the Chinese Government's special envoy for the Middle East, Zhai Jun, is currently in the region carrying out visits as part of Beijing's mediation efforts, and indicated that the Chinese authorities will inform “in due course” about their agenda, without offering further details about the mission's objectives or itinerary. The Chinese Foreign Ministry only reported that Zhai arrived in Saudi Arabia on Sunday, where he met with Foreign Minister Faisal bin Farhan. According to Guo, the handling of the Iranian issue and other issues related to the Middle East must be based on principles such as “respecting national sovereignty”, “refraining from abuse of force” and “maintaining non-interference in internal affairs”. The spokesperson added that China will continue to maintain communication with the parties involved and strengthen mediation efforts to “promote a de-escalation of the situation”. Energy security The official added that China will adopt the “necessary measures” to safeguard its energy security and called for “stable and fluid” energy supplies to be guaranteed. Guo Jiakun said that energy security is “crucial for the world economy” and stated that all countries have a responsibility to ensure supply stability. The repercussions of the conflict in the Strait of Hormuz directly affect China, which receives around 45 percent of the oil it imports from Iran and several other countries. Guo added that Beijing will take measures to protect its energy security, although he did not specify the concrete policies the country will implement. The spokesperson also highlighted that the Strait of Hormuz and the surrounding waters are “important channels for international trade in goods and energy”, adding that maintaining security and stability in that area “responds to the common interest of the international community”. China, Tehran's main trading partner and its biggest oil buyer, has repeatedly condemned attacks on Iran by the United States and Israel for “violating the sovereignty” of the Persian country.

item.title: Prison | Guard accepted bribes to let goods in
item.description:
The main target of the CCAC investigation is said to have received “at least” 17 thousand patacas to let products into the prison, which were then sold by an inmate, who profited from the scheme
The Commissioner against Corruption (CCAC) announced that it had discovered the case of a prison guard from the Directorate of Correctional Services (DSC) who accepted bribes to help an inmate “smuggle products into the prison establishment”. The investigation also focused on another prison guard, who is currently retired. “Following the investigation, the CCAC verified that the active prison guard had taken advantage of his duties to help an inmate smuggle products into the prison area”, it was announced. When the goods reached the inmate, he would sell them inside the prison to profit from illegality. “The inmate in question paid, through his wife, to the prison guard an amount of at least 17 thousand patacas in return for his help”, it was explained. This prison guard is suspected of committing the crimes of passive corruption for an illicit act, which carries a penalty of up to eight years in prison, and the crime of document forgery, which carries a penalty of up to three years in prison. The investigation did not stop at bribery or contraband inside the prison. The authorities also announced that the prison guard “had already committed speeding offenses twice”. However, it was discovered that the guard, “to avoid responsibility”, “had resorted to a family member and a friend to take the blame”. The family members and friend, who according to the investigation were used as scapegoats, are suspected of committing the crime of document forgery, the penalty for which can be up to three years in prison.
Other involved In relation to the second prison guard investigated, who is now retired, it was indicated that “he provided assistance, during the time he was carrying out his duties, to another inmate in smuggling products into the prison establishment”. This prison guard was also investigated for having “helped” the inmate “to exchange messages, secretly and on several occasions, with his family”. In view of the investigation, the retiree is suspected of committing the crime of abuse of power, punishable by a prison sentence of up to three years. According to the CCAC, the cases were forwarded to the Public Ministry and communicated to the DSC. Reacting to the investigation, the DSC stated that it was “deeply saddened by the fact that some prison guards have knowingly broken the law and violated their duties, seriously damaging the reputation of the prison police”. DSC further indicated that “it takes this matter very seriously and will fully cooperate with the judicial authorities in the criminal investigation”. “DSC reiterates that it has zero tolerance for any illegal or disciplinary breaches by any member of staff and will deal with them seriously, in accordance with the law, in order to uphold the rule of law and maintain the reputation and credibility of the correctional service,” it added.

item.title: Paradise Entertainment | Alert for reduced profits
item.description: Paradise Entertainment, which managed the Kam Pek satellite casino and provides gaming machines, warned investors of a 63.5 percent profit reduction in 2025 to HK$139.4 million. The warning was left in a statement sent to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, which states that part of the reduction in profits was due to payments made to SJM related to workers at the Kam Pek satellite casino, which closed last year. “This decrease in profits was mainly due […] to the group's payments to employees and reimbursements to SJM Resorts, related to employers contracted by SJM Resorts who worked for Kam Pek Paradise Casino,” it was stated. These payments totaled 42.1 million Hong Kong dollars. Among the factors that contributed to the reduction in profits, the company also highlighted a decrease of 85.2 million Hong Kong dollars in revenue from the provision of casino management services in Macau and a reduction of 165.5 million Hong Kong dollars in the sale of gaming equipment. In 2024, the company responsible for operating Kam Pek had recorded profits of 245.5 million Hong Kong dollars.

item.title: Concert | Kiri T premieres live on Saturday at the Broadway Theater
item.description:
The Broadway Theater will be the first stage in Macau that Kiri T will perform on. The debut concert is scheduled for Saturday and the singer, one of the main voices of the new generation of Hong Kong artists, promises to create an intimate atmosphere that frees her from shyness.
One of the most interesting voices of the new generation of Hong Kong singers makes his debut on Saturday in Macau. Kiri T's first concert in the territory is scheduled for the Broadway Theater at 8 p.m. Yesterday there were still tickets for seats furthest from the stage, which can be purchased at the online box office for 680 patacas. The concert, titled “Hello, Introvert” highlights the atmosphere that the artist wants to create at the Broadway Theatre: a sanctuary and “intimate place of emotional refuge, where introverts can release, connect and heal in freedom through music”, describes the organization of the show by the Hong Kong artist. Since appearing on the music scene in the neighboring region, Kiri T (Kiri Tse Hiu Ying) has been gaining dedicated fans, despite the wandering way in which he jumps between musical genres with each record, moving from traditional cantopop to more intimate indie sounds, or more expansive emo rock, with soulful detours into R&B or forays into electronic music. The ability to transform herself has earned Kiri T a reputation as an artist with expanding artistic vision and expressiveness. “Fluent in Cantonese, English and Mandarin, the singer transitions easily between languages, combining refined vocal control with emotional sincerity to create songs that resonate deeply”, describes the organization. Despite the creative frenzy, Kiri T created an introverted persona focused on writing songs that resemble emotional diaries, with quiet and honest reflections on love and everyday life, offering moments of recognition and comfort in whispered ballads.
A gentileza dos milhões A popularity phenomenon, Kiri T has accumulated millions of streams on platforms such as Spotify or YouTube. For example, the music video for the song “You Gotta Screw Up At Least Once”, released around two years ago, has already surpassed 11 million views on YouTube and 15 million streams on Spotify. On her Spotify profile, Kiri T states that she grew up between Hong Kong, the United Kingdom and the United States. "I love beautiful melodies, lush instrumentation and lyrics that cut deep. I suffer from insane anxiety, but puppies make everything better." This is how the singer describes herself. Kiri T's career started very early. At the age of 14, he was already composing songs for Cantopop diva Joey Yung. Before reaching the age of 20, and with some songs written, Kiri T signed with Denise Ho's Goomusic label. In 2015, he released his first single under his own name “Twenty-Something”, which ended up being remixed by Lucian, surpassing 7 million streams on Spotify. This exhibition lit the spark that exploded the young artist's career. Among singles released in English or Cantonese, in 2019 Kiri T's debut album “Golden Kiri” was released, the first of three albums in two years. In 2022, the singer won the awards for best singer and best song at the Whats Good Music Awards, with the recognition being extended to other music awards. João Luz

item.title: Economy | Request return of consumption coupons
item.description:
The Macau Industrial and Commercial Association defends a hybrid economy, with periods in which SMEs live based on an increase in tourists, in high seasons, and support for consumption, during low seasons.
Given the reduction in the number of tourists in Macau, post-Lunar New Year, the executive president of the Macau Industrial and Commercial Association, Ng Wah Wai, defended more measures to encourage domestic consumption, such as discount coupons. Ng's position was shared in statements cited by the Ou Mun newspaper. According to the Macau Industrial and Commercial Association, discount coupons are an efficient way to promote the economy during periods when there are fewer tourists in the territory. Ng defended a hybrid economy, with periods in which Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) can survive solely on additional income from tourists, during high seasons, and others in which, faced with the reduction in visitors, SMEs receive help from consumption coupons. Ng Wah Wai also warned that the number of visitors to Macau will continue to gradually decrease in the coming months, so the distribution of coupons is vital to boost the community economy, and alleviate the concerns of SMEs. The executive president of the Macau Industrial and Commercial Association also called on the Executive to consider allowing the use of coupons in more sectors, in addition to restaurants and retail, and to increase the amount directly allocated to the elderly. New models The possibility of distributing more consumer coupons was also discussed by Matthew Liu Ting Chi, professor at the Faculty of Business Management at the University of Macau. Liu defended the coupon program, considering that from a macroeconomic and microeconomic perspective these measures to encourage consumption effectively bring new vitality to the market. However, the academic suggested that the Executive consider changing the model used in recent editions of allocating coupons. Until now, this type of program has worked with coupons that can be obtained through a draw, based on consumption on weekdays. The coupons can then be used on the weekend immediately following the draw, and only on those days. Liu Ting Chi suggests a new model in which people can only qualify for coupons when they consume on the weekend, and can then use the discounts during weekdays. The academic also asked for a longer period for the use of coupons, beyond the weekend immediately following the award, to allow for new consumption patterns. In this way, the UM academic believes that consumption will cover more sectors, in addition to supermarkets, pharmacies and restaurants, the sectors most benefited by previous measures.

item.title: IAM | Portuguese sidewalks can be made of non-slip material
item.description:
Portuguese sidewalks outside historic areas can be replaced with non-slip material, maintaining the original pattern and design, according to a person responsible for the Institute for Municipal Affairs. In historic areas, authorities will maintain the sidewalk, one of the city's distinctive elements
The Portuguese sidewalk can be replaced with another type of flooring, non-slip, but maintaining the same type of pattern and aesthetics. The idea was put forward yesterday by Ng Chi Lun, from the Department of Public Roads and Sanitation of the Institute for Municipal Affairs (IAM), during the Forum Macau program on the Chinese channel Rádio Macau. The official stated that his department will continue to inspect and optimize Portuguese sidewalks in the city's historic areas, as well as in buffer zones, adjacent to areas subject to heritage protection. The main theme of the day was the city's cleaning campaign, which will advance at the end of the month. According to IAM president Chao Wai Ieng, this year's campaign will be more comprehensive than last year's, and will last longer. "We launched the campaign for the first time last year, with the participation of various public services, associations and institutions, totaling around 50 thousand people. We set up consultation and exhibition points in residential neighborhoods and raised the population's awareness of maintaining hygienic and clean urban environments, and combating rats", said the president of IAM. Reach further Chao Wai Ieng also stated that this year the IAM intends to increase the frequency of cleaning actions in buildings without a condominium assembly, residents' organization or a company that takes care of the administration. Last year, garbage was the cause of the vast majority of complaints received by the City Beautification and Cleaning Working Group, which followed up on 7,489 complaints regarding the hygiene and beauty of the streets. Of these nearly 7,500 cases, about 97 percent have been resolved. Also last year, the IAM punished around 15 thousand infractions for hygienic reasons, more than half related to the accumulation of rubbish on public roads. As for offenses committed by tourists, the most frequent were throwing rubbish on the ground or spitting. The head of the IAM's Environmental Hygiene and Licensing Department, in response to a listener, stated that Taipa Central Park stands out as one of the places targeted for the most complaints due to the presence of dog feces.

item.title: Shuttle Bus | Government says it asked operators for more services
item.description:
After the controversy over the lack of alternatives to ensure condominium shuttle services, the Government defends itself and indicates that it has asked public bus operators to provide more vehicles
Regarding the difficulties faced by residential buildings in providing shuttle services, the Government indicates that it has asked public transport operators to provide more buses. The explanations appear in a response to an interpellation by deputy Chan Hao Weng, which is signed by Chan U Tong, director of the Services for Labor Affairs (DSAL). The difficulties in contracting bus services by condominiums in large housing complexes became a concern for deputies, with the entry into force of the law on the activity of travel agencies and the profession of tourist guide, which prohibits travel agencies from providing this type of transport service. Now the Government says that it has asked public bus operators to make more buses available. “The Macao SAR Government, through interdepartmental coordination, promoted the rental of their ‘non-operational vehicles’ to the two bus operators to provide transport services, with a view to meeting the travel needs of residents of the housing complexes in question”, it was stated. At the same time, the Government argued that it requested more buses to run near the buildings: “DSAT also asked bus operators to increase attention to the waiting situation at stops around the complexes, increasing the frequency of services in a timely manner to better transport the flow of passengers”, it was added. Despite the response, in which the director of DSAL cites DSAT's position, the issue of increasing transport costs is not addressed by government officials. Previous ban As for the ban on travel agency buses being hired for this shuttle service, the Government insists that the measure was in force even before the new law, despite the widespread practice. “Between 2015 and 2025, there were more than 140 procedures initiated against travel agencies that violated the rules regarding the use of vehicles, including the illegal provision of tourist buses as buses for workers or as buses connecting to housing complexes”, it was indicated. The numbers presented represent an average of 12.7 “procedures initiated” per year. “As a supervisory entity, DST has supervised, under the terms of the law, the activity of travel agencies, and as long as the illegal provision of services by travel agencies is verified, the respective procedure will be initiated”, it was stressed.

item.title: Technology | Woman excited by robot received medical assistance
item.description:
The CPSP reported that the humanoid robot was controlled by a man aged around 50, who aims to commercialize this type of equipment in the future
The Public Security Police Corps (CPSP) announced that the woman who was seen arguing with a humanoid robot had to receive medical treatment after being frightened. The information was provided in a response sent to HM. "Around 9pm on March 5th, the Public Security Police received a report that a woman needed help outside a building on Rua do Sul, in S. Lázaro. The agents immediately went to the location", it was indicated. "It was found that the woman, while using her cell phone, was startled when she realized that a robot was approaching from behind. There was no physical contact between the woman and the robot, nor did she suffer any injuries. However, she reported feeling unwell and required hospital treatment", it was added. On Monday night, the woman had already been discharged, but chose not to file any type of complaint against the robot's owner. “She has been discharged and has not taken any legal action in relation to the incident,” it was reported. The CPSP also revealed that the robot was returned to the owner, after being advised to use it carefully. The same source clarified that the robot was not operated by artificial intelligence: “The robot involved was operated by a local man in his fifties, who stated that he was carrying out tests at the time, with the intention of using it for commercial promotions in the future”, it was pointed out. “Police reminded the man that when operating robots, he must remain constantly aware of the situation to avoid causing danger or alarm to the surrounding environment or passersby,” it added. Regulation requests This was the second public incident with a humanoid robot in the territory in recent weeks. Also in the first case, the robot was being tested and then commercialized, according to Zeng Zengwei, member of the North Zone Community Services Advisory Council. At the most recent meeting of this council, this member made a request to the authorities to implement new regulations to deal with robots, mainly given the possibility of them being controlled by artificial intelligence and due to image and sound capture equipment, which could jeopardize the protection of personal data.

item.title: Hutchison | Sale of “3” in Macau yielded 110 million
item.description: The sale of “3” was explained by the losses of the operator linked to Hutchison Telecom, which reached 43 million Hong Kong dollars. Macau's telecommunications market is now entirely concentrated in Chinese state-owned companies CITIC and China Telecom The sale of telecommunications operator “3” by Hutchison Telecom to Macau Telecommunications Company (CTM) netted the Hong Kong company 110 million Hong Kong dollars. The numbers appear in the company's annual results, released in a statement to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on Monday. “In January 2026, the Group completed the sale of all its interests in 3 Macau for a value of 110 million Hong Kong dollars, ending more than two decades of operations in the Macau market”, was explained by Canning Fok Kin Ning, president of Hutchison Telecom, which is part of the group founded by Li Ka-shing. “The sale will serve to support sustained improvements in the group’s profitability in the coming years, allowing it to redistribute resources more effectively and further simplify its operational structure,” added the president. The document also shows that the company's losses in Macau had been increasing in recent years. In 2025, losses in Macau were 43 million Hong Kong dollars, a figure that increased 72 percent compared to the previous year, when losses in Macau were 25 million Hong Kong dollars. This is a difference of 18 million Hong Kong dollars, which had a significant impact on the group's results. The year 2025 closed for Hutchison Telecom with losses of 25 million Hong Kong dollars, when the previous year it had recorded a profit of 6 million. Monopoly regime With the sale of Hutchison to CTM, the market is formally left with three operators, as the 3 brand will be maintained for now, but CTM and 3 will be controlled by the same shareholders, essentially the CITIC Group, a Chinese state-owned company. The other operator in the Macau market is also a Chinese state-owned company, in this case China Telecom. This situation has raised concerns about a possible weakening of the clients' position, as was exposed by the association Centro da Políticas da Sabedoria Colectiva, through the vice-president of Loi Man Keong. However, the Government dismissed any concerns, devaluing the concentration and promised to continue carrying out regularization functions. “This transfer of shareholdings essentially involves a change in the shareholders of Hutchison, which continues to operate its network and provide services”, was indicated in a statement from the Postal and Telecommunications Services Department (CTT). “In other words, currently, the market continues to have three mobile telecommunications operators, and the Government continues to strictly monitor Hutchison's compliance with its license obligations, ensuring the provision of stable mobile telecommunications services to users”, it was added.

item.title: Pharmacies | ISAF calls for promoting the physical and mental health of the population
item.description:
The president of the Institute for Pharmaceutical Supervision and Administration wants more participation by pharmacies in promoting the physical and mental health of the population. Choi Peng Cheong also advocates greater integration of the culture of traditional Chinese medicine into the daily lives of residents
The president of the Institute for Pharmaceutical Supervision and Administration (ISAF), Choi Peng Cheong, wants Macau's pharmacies to gain a greater role in promoting the physical and mental health of the population. The position was taken during a series of talks with the pharmaceutical sector organized by ISAF, according to a statement released yesterday by the organization. Choi Peng Cheong also pointed to the role that Macau pharmacists can play in caring for the elderly, as well as in supervising the market. The president of ISAF framed the appeal to the sector within the “governmental concept of decentralization of service resources to community neighborhoods”, giving priority to care for the elderly and children, designated as “key groups”. The leader launched a challenge to pharmacists to take the initiative to pay particular attention to patients who take “long-term medication, polypharmacy and potential health risks. Regarding traditional Chinese medicine pharmacists, Choi Peng Cheong recalled that this is one of the priority areas in economic diversification policies, but that they must have a more present intervention in the community, transmitting knowledge about prevention and health care. The official argues that in Macau there must be “integration of the culture of traditional Chinese medicine into daily life”, “fostering the physical and mental health of residents”. Main role The central idea of ​​the ISAF president's appeal is to reinforce the “role and function of Chinese community pharmacies and pharmacies in safeguarding and promoting public health”, enhancing the social value of the pharmaceutical profession and its proximity to people. "Macau's pharmacies and Chinese pharmacies are spread throughout the city, forming an important primary pharmaceutical care network; community pharmacists are one of the most accessible and trusted professionals among residents, playing an indispensable role in promoting public health," he said. During the talks, topics such as the development of young pharmaceutical professionals and the response to the aging population were also discussed. Representatives of associations linked to the sector communicated to the authorities the current situation of their businesses and asked questions about market supervision methods.

item.title: Exhibition | Watercolors by Hana Tou Mei Kun at Fantasia 10 until April 29
item.description: The exhibition “Watercolors of Hana Tou Mei Kun” is on display in the Fantasia building, on Calçada da Igreja de São Lázaro, until April 29th. The exhibition brings together more than 30 recent pieces by the young local artist, “with emphasis on watercolor landscapes, along with sketches and works in acrylic and tile paintings”. According to curator Leong Kit Man, Hana Tou Mei Kun's paintings “capture the serene beauty of a gentle breeze and, like the painting, so is the painter”. The exhibition curator describes the artist as “an empathetic, kind and caring art teacher who cultivated countless young artistic talents”. Hana Tou Mei Kun was a student of local artist Dor Lio Hak Man and continued her studies in Taiwan, at National Chiayi University and Ming Chuan University. Currently, she is an art teacher Reflecting on her years of practice, the artist reveals that “she has always felt attracted to watercolor landscapes, with the quick and free flow of brushstrokes inspiring her great joy”, describes the organization of the exhibition. “Watercolors of Hana Tou Mei Kun” is organized by the Macau Young Art Association, in collaboration with the Promotion Association for Creative Industries in the Parish of São Lázaro, 10 Fantasia and the Macau Art Teachers Communication Association. The exhibition can be visited daily, between 11am and 6pm, except on Mondays.

item.title: Japanese company fails to launch the country's first private satellite
item.description: Space One failed yesterday for the third consecutive time in its attempt to become the first Japanese company to put satellites into orbit. The Kairos III rocket managed to take off at 11:10 local time, as scheduled, from Wakayama prefecture, in western Japan, but was forced to abort the launch mission shortly after takeoff. Just five minutes after takeoff, the Japanese company announced the decision to abort the mission, without providing further information for now, while it continues to “investigate the details” of what happened, according to a statement from Space One. The launch of the rocket, 18 meters high, had already been postponed three times, the last time on Wednesday, due to a failure in connections with the satellites it was carrying. The objective of the mission was to place five satellites into orbit at around 500 kilometers, according to Japanese public broadcaster NHK. If it had succeeded, the company would have become the first in the country to place a satellite in orbit, a milestone that, for now, remains to be achieved. History of failures Space One launched the first Kairos rocket in March 2024, but the device exploded about five minutes after takeoff due to an error in predicting the thrust. In December of the same year, it launched the second device, Kairos II, which self-destructed after those responsible for the project concluded that it would be difficult to carry out the mission due to problems with the sensors. Founded in 2018, the company seeks to commercialize low-cost space transportation services and offer regular rocket launches exclusively with private funds. The failure represents a new setback for the Japanese space industry's efforts and ambitions to compete on a global level with companies such as North American SpaceX.

item.title: Game | Citigroup highlights “solid start” to March
item.description: March got off to a “solid start” in terms of gaming revenue, according to the latest report from investment bank Citigroup. The document is cited by the GGRAsia portal. “Based on our industry sources, we found that Macau’s gross gaming revenues in the first eight days of March reached around MOP5.65 billion,” stated in the report signed by George Choi and Timothy Chau. “This implies an average daily revenue of around 706 million patacas, around 4 percent lower than the measure of gross gaming revenue in February 2026 – 737 million patacas per day –, but around 11 percent higher than that of March 2025, of 634 million patacas per day”, it was added. According to Citi, the various shows organized by casino concessionaires at the beginning of March contributed to the annual increase in gaming revenue. “We believe that the concerts by Korean boy band Treasure at the Venetian Arena, Chinese singer-songwriter Wang Sulong and Korean girl group Aespa at Arena Galaxy may have contributed to the solid start to March 2026”, it was pointed out. The good numbers still represent an expected reduction in revenue compared to February, which is normally one of the game's peak seasons, as it coincides with the Lunar New Year. “Based on industry sources, VIP volumes fell between 2 percent and 4 percent compared to the previous month, and mass revenues fell between 3 percent and 5 percent compared to the previous month,” they noted.

item.title: Ana Cristina Alves
item.description:
Ana Cristina Alves – Assistant Researcher and Coordinator of the Educational Service of the Macau Scientific and Cultural Center The popular myth of origin “Pangu Separates the Heaven” (盘古开天Pángǔ kāi tiān) dates back to the Three Kingdoms Period (三国, 220-280) and its Three-Five Calendar Record (《三五历纪》)2, according to data from the online encyclopedia Baike.Baidu, where the Hun Tian cosmological theory (“浑天说”) is exposed, which means “theory of the Celestial Globe” or, in a less literal translation, “theory of the cosmic egg””3: The Three Five Calendar Record reads "Heaven and Earth were linked like an egg, giving rise to Pangu. In Hun Tian theory, the celestial sphere surrounds the earth, and the center where Heaven and Earth meet is known as 'the geographic center'. It was in Luoyang that Pangu was born, at that time Heaven and Earth had not yet separated, the universe was a chaotic mixture. Pangu slept in this chaos for 18 thousand years. One day, suddenly He woke up. All around him, there was pitch darkness. He raised a large ax with which he fought against the darkness. It dissipated with an immense noise and gradually became the Earth. After Heaven and Earth had separated, the titan feared that they would unite again, so that this would never be possible, he placed his head against Heaven, leaving his feet firmly planted on Earth. And that's how Heaven rose upwards, moving three meters away from Earth every day. For many years he too followed the growth of Heaven, ever higher and more distant. When Heaven and Earth were fully formed, Pangu fell exhausted. After his fall, his body underwent the greatest changes: His breath became the wind and clouds of the four seasons; the voice in thunderous thunder; eyes on the sun and moon, the four cardinal points of the Earth, east, west, south and north; the muscles and skin across the vast expanse of the Earth; the blood in small and large rivers always flowing and the sweat, in the rain and dew that nourish the ten thousand beings. 据《三五历纪》记载，“天地浑沌如鸡子，盘古生其中” 。浑天说中，球壳状的天包着地，天与地的中心就是“地中”，即洛阳，盘古生于此。当时天和地还没有分开，宇宙一片。盘古在这个混沌的宇宙中睡了一万八千年。有一天，盘古突然醒了。他见周围一片漆黑，他就抡起大斧头，朝眼前的黑暗猛劈过去了。只听一声巨响，一片黑暗的东西渐渐分散开了。轻清的东西缓缓上升，变成了天；重浊的东西慢慢下降，变成了地。 天和地分开以后，盘古怕它们还会合在一起，便头顶着天，脚蹬着地。天每天升高一丈，盘古也随着天越长越高。这样不知过多少年，天和地逐渐成形了，盘古也累得倒下来了。 盘古倒下后，他呼出的气息，变成了四季的风和云；他发出的声音，化作了隆隆的雷声:的四肢, Article
João Marcelo Martins in A Gramática Universal do Mito (2023, 142) draws attention to the various etymological meanings of the name of Pangu (盘古), which, in a possible reading, is close to the phonetics of the word «cabaça», Páohù (匏瓠), which in Chinese culture has the symbolic meaning of reproduction of life, there are still other interpretative possibilities surrounding the name of this great titan, such as that of a «spiral antiquity» (Ibidem), because one of the sinograms is 古 (gǔ), which means «ancient», while the other 盘 (pán), can be translated as «spiral». The truth is that this Chinese myth of the cosmic egg is found in several mythologies, such as the Orphic mythology of ancient Greece, which emphasizes the image of a large egg from which all the beings of the universe sprouted, like chicks, including the very bases of it – Heaven and Earth. An identical panorama presents us with the Chinese genesis myth. In this creation myth, we are given an image that appeals to a chaotic mixture, in which the universe is immersed in total darkness. Afterwards, we witness the sacrifice of the titan, who manages to separate and constitute the two basic forces, representatives of the masculine principle Yang (阳), Heaven, and the feminine principle Yin (阴), the earth. All of creation is an act of sacrifice of the portentous, which begins in the thousands of years in which the divine force separates the elements and culminates in the exhaustion of the titan. It is from Pangu's body and, through transformation, that all living beings, celestial and telluric, emerge. There is an immanent, living and animated energy that keeps a universe connected, as if in one body. The generous sacrifices don’t stop there. Neither in China, nor in ancient Greece, do they become very clear when one thinks about the descriptions found for the creation of humanity by the Goddess Nϋwa (女娲), that in some version of the myth “Nϋwa creates human beings” (女娲造人Nǚ wā zào rén) after creating humanity with the help of the earth and filling it with people, she falls deeply asleep, such was the state of tiredness and one is left without knowing if he will not have woken up in paradise never to return to the world. The Goddess, after creating the animals, continued to feel alone, so "on the seventh day, she arrived at a lake. She took some clay and mixed it with water. When she saw her reflected image, she made a piece of clay similar to her" (Wang, Alves, 2009, 16). To make it move, it was enough to place the doll and all the others that would follow, in contact with the earth. The energy of this animated them, being in itself indispensable in the transition from dolls to the creation of humanity. As happened in the ancient Greek world, the cosmos arises from the void and primordial Chaos, then, through spontaneous generation, as stated in Hesiod's theogony, Mother-Earth Gaia, Eros, the God of Love, manifests itself and from this all the deified elements: the Sky or Uranus, the Sea and the Mountains, etc. Gaia, with the help of Uranus, generates the remaining children, some of them colossal and deformed creatures like the Cyclops, with fifty heads and one hundred arms, in addition to the titans, the giants, as well as the remaining gods, which Cronus, the god of time called titans, which meant in his idea “arrogant and ambitious creatures”. The Greek Gods had all human defects and virtues, they were evil, jealous, vengeful, but also kind and giving, depending on the way they behaved on their moods. In ancient Greece, humanity was not forged in the likeness of the gods, but, on the contrary, the gods were conceived in the image and likeness of humanity. Poor those divine forces that out of love for humanity tried to deceive their peers, as happened with the titan Prometheus who went to Olympus to steal fire from the gods to provide humanity with a powerful weapon. The episode took place after Epimetheus, Prometheus' younger brother, whose name means “what he thinks after”, endowed the animals, when he created them with the best virtues that he took from the saddlebag prepared by the younger gods: claws, fangs, wings, tentacles, beaks, stingers, speed and everything else that animals have. Prometheus, whose name means “foresighted thinker”, upon concluding that there was nothing left for himself on earth that would distinguish humanity, went to Olympus, deceiving and extorting the fire of the gods, thus endowing human beings with intelligence so that they could defend themselves from animals with physical capabilities far superior to theirs. To do so, he had to catch Hestia, the virgin goddess of the home, distracted, but the same did not happen to Zeus, the supreme leader of the gods, who was not fooled. He punished what he considered to be the supreme arrogance of the titan thief with a terrible torture. He shackled Prometheus in the Caucasus Mountains and ordered Hephaestus, god of forging and other arts, to make shackles to chain him in his furnace. The creator of humanity was trapped by Power and Force on a cliff, with arms and legs stretched out, held by the wrists and ankles, being visited daily by a voracious eagle, which tore apart his diaphragm and ate his liver. Aeschylus (525- 456 BC), the great Greek playwright, recounts the torture, inspired by Hesiod's Theogony, in the tragedy Prometheus Bound, leaving the hero to unburden himself at the end of the play that recounts his adventure in the gift of fire to Humanity: (…) Here’s the gust who, to bring terror upon me, It was sent by Zeus. Oh Majesty! Oh my mother! Divine ether! ― That, across the world beyond, phases roll wonderful hymn: the Light – supreme good, to illuminate the tragic fate from everyone! See how great my misfortune is, The pain that tortures me - Unfairly!… (Aeschylus, 1946, 115) Prometheus' sacrifice will have an echo in the Christian world, in which the son of God, Jesus Christ, also sacrifices himself for the love of Humanity. He dies on the cross to save men, who invariably behave badly. But there is a big difference in the way Chinese and Christians view the cosmos, with ancient Greece establishing the bridge between both worldviews. From the Christian perspective, God created the world out of nothing (Creatio ex nihilo). It is a transcendent, personal creation with the artist's signature. The fruit of creation may not have been the best, but it will always remain unexplained how evil entered the world, even with Saint Augustine defending the theory of human free will. Here is the account of creation ex nihilo in the book of Genesis of the Old Testament (Gen 1): In the beginning God created Heaven and Earth. The Earth was formless and empty; darkness covered the abyss and a fierce wind blew over the waters. God said: “Let there be light!” And the light began to exist. God saw that the light was good. God separated the light from the darkness: the light God called “day” and the darkness he called “night”. There was an afternoon and a morning, it was the first day. Then followed the creation of the firmament, which God called heaven on the second day. On the third day, he created the earth, the sea and the plants. On the fourth day the sun, moon and stars were created. On the fifth day, the fish, the birds and all living beings. On the sixth day, animals were divided into species and man was created. On the seventh day he rested. Regarding the creation of humanity, God in one version creates them directly and in his image and likeness (Gn 1, 27-31), in the other he creates first the man and then the woman from his rib (Gn 2,18-23). After which, a long road is covered until we reach the New Testament and the sacrifice of Jesus Christ, who died on the cross for the love of humanity. There is a big difference, from a philosophical point of view, between the Chinese and Greek immanentist cosmologies and the Christian transcendent one. The first obey the principle of transformation of elements that have always been immersed in primordial chaos and, therefore, present an interrelated cosmological image, in which the elements or ten thousand beings have root affinities that allow them to communicate and transmute themselves whenever necessary. Greek gods, for example, take whatever forms they wish, just like Chinese deities and immortals. In the Christian West, the absence of a cosmic egg or primordial chaos hinders and prevents transmutation. Beings are all separate and in an external position in relation to divinity, since if one is made in the image of god, one is not the divine himself and to achieve this status a mystical union with him is supposed. The profane exterior, split, distant will have to travel a long path, which perhaps involves martyrdom and sacrifice already experienced by the son of God. In any case, even within the scope of immanentist philosophies, guided by the principle of transformation, sacrifice is also always present, when combined with love for humanity, as in the case of Prometheus or, in part, of Nϋwa; or without love, but by natural essence, when the alliance is made over time, if the example of Pangu is considered. The gods are sacrificed, but they are not alone. The sacrifice is universal, because natural creatures are also martyred for love or time in the three cosmogonies presented, they immolate themselves to deities, some more distant, others so close that they can only be intuited from within. References Holy Bible. 1997. São Paulo: Paulus. Squirrel. 1946. Prometheus Fettered. Translated by Eduardo Scarlatti. Lisbon: Livraria Luso-Espanhola, Lda “浑天说” (Hun Tian theory) In Baidu. Baike. https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%B5%91%E5%A4%A9%E8%AF%B4/717090?fromModule=lemma_inlink, accessed February 25, 2026. “盘古开天” (Pangu separates Heaven). In Baidu. Baike. https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E7%9B%98%E5%8F%A4%E5%BC%80%E5%A4%A9/74007，2008年6月7日, accessed February 24, 2026. Johnston, Sarah Iles. 2025. Gods and Mortals. Odivelas: Soul of Books Martins, João Cancelo. 2023. The Universal Grammar of Myth: Comparative and Contrastive Analysis of Chinese Myths of Origin. Lisbon: Macau Scientific and Cultural Center, University of Macau. Wang Suoying, Ana Cristina Alves. 2009. Myths and Legends of the land of the Dragon. Lisbon: Path. Notes: I This space has the collaboration of the Macau Scientific and Cultural Center, in Lisbon, and the opinions expressed in the article are the sole responsibility of the authors. According to scholar João Martins in The Universal Grammar of Myth. Comparative and Contrastive Analysis of Chinese Origin Myths (2023, 143, note 100) The Three Five Calendar concerns the now lost historical records of the Three Sovereign Deities and the Five Gods. iii The Hun Tian theory, founded by Zhang Heng (张衡), an astronomer from the Han Dynasty, is an astronomical theory between the geocentric and heliocentric theses, in which it is argued that the universe is infinite, the movement of celestial bodies is regular, moonlight is the reflection of sunlight and lunar eclipses are caused by the Earth blocking sunlight. To prove his Hun Tian Theory, Zhang Heng built the first “water-powered armillary sphere”, ushering in an era of more accurate astronomical observation. He then used more rigorous astronomical observation data to corroborate his theory in explaining various celestial phenomena, influencing astronomy for more than 1500 years. (cf https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%B5%91%E5%A4%A9%E8%AF%B4/717090?fromModule=lemma_inlink)

item.title: Paulo Maia and Carmo
item.description: Shen Deqian (1673-1769), whom Emperor Qianlong (r. 1736-95) called the "illustrious old scholar of Jiangnan", was a notable and influential high official, poet, essayist and master of thought with a career marked by successive surprises, such as the unusual fact that he only passed the difficult jinshi exam in 1739, after seventeen attempts and when he had already sixty-seven years. But soon after, he would assume important roles as deputy minister in the Ministry of Rites or Grand Tutor of the Royal Prince. A role that he had long performed for private individuals. The recognition that Qianlong showed him included not only the «imperial favor» that allowed him to stroll in the imperial gardens but also the rare privilege of having a temple erected in his name for which the emperor wrote with his brush the phrase: «Venerable dean of the world of poetry». Many decrees and memorials were written with the authority that possessed his brush, such as the one that states that in 1699, “Emperor Kangxi declared that from now on, Spring Dawn on the Su (Shi) Embankment will be the first name on the list of the Ten Views of the West Lake.” At the time of his death, the emperor himself composed an elegy for him. No one would deny the master's persistent and valiant exemplary career. But suddenly in 1778, he had already been dead for eight years, his honorary titles were taken away, his commemorative temple was abolished, his posthumous name was revoked, his tombstone was torn down, his body was even exhumed and quartered, among other punishments. And all because of the reference to a flower. During a period of literary inquisitions, a subtle reference to the perenniality of the previous dynasty was discovered in a work by Xu Shukui (1703-1763). That Shen Deqian, his biographer and fellow countryman from Jiangsu, where there was unstoppable resistance to Manchu power, praised and quoted in the verses: «Nothing but the color red can be the true one,/ Even if foreign varieties proclaim themselves «queen of flowers» (huawang, a popular name for peony). Both were severely condemned, even post-mortem. Wang Jiu (1745-98), a painter who Wang Yu included in his Oriental Estate Painting Manual at the end of the 18th century, Dongzhuang hualun, as one of the Four Little Wangs, recalled verses by Shen Deqian in the year before his conviction, in a small album (ink on paper, 13.8 x 20.5 cm, for sale at Christie's). On one of the pages there is just a willow tree, an undulating emblem of longing, and beneath it a fisherman in a boat evoking the desire to sail free over the danger of the waters. Which sometimes came only in an allusion to red peonies, and could even have a brutal punishment, but if one was skillful, as Shen Deqian wrote about Meng Haoran's poetry, «the language will be simple, but the flavor will remain.»

item.title: Today Macau
item.description: The Chinese Government stated yesterday that it is developing “active mediation” in the conflict in the Middle East and that it will continue to maintain contacts with the parties involved to promote the return to negotiations. Foreign Ministry spokesman Guo Jiakun said at a press conference that China is “deeply concerned” about tensions and called on all parties to cease military operations to avoid further escalation of the conflict. Guo recalled that the head of Chinese diplomacy, Wang Yi, had recent contacts with several counterparts in the region to “exchange in-depth opinions on the regional situation”. The spokesperson added that the Chinese Government's special envoy for the Middle East, Zhai Jun, is currently in the region carrying out visits as part of Beijing's mediation efforts, and indicated that the Chinese authorities will inform “in due course” about their agenda, without offering further details about the mission's objectives or itinerary. The Chinese Foreign Ministry only reported that Zhai arrived in Saudi Arabia on Sunday, where he met with Foreign Minister Faisal bin Farhan. According to Guo, the handling of the Iranian issue and other issues related to the Middle East must be based on principles such as “respecting national sovereignty”, “refraining from abuse of force” and “maintaining non-interference in internal affairs”. The spokesperson added that China will continue to maintain communication with the parties involved and strengthen mediation efforts to “promote a de-escalation of the situation”. Energy security The official added that China will adopt the “necessary measures” to safeguard its energy security and called for “stable and fluid” energy supplies to be guaranteed. Guo Jiakun said that energy security is “crucial for the world economy” and stated that all countries have a responsibility to ensure supply stability. The repercussions of the conflict in the Strait of Hormuz directly affect China, which receives around 45 percent of the oil it imports from Iran and several other countries. Guo added that Beijing will take measures to protect its energy security, although he did not specify the concrete policies the country will implement. The spokesperson also highlighted that the Strait of Hormuz and the surrounding waters are “important channels for international trade in goods and energy”, adding that maintaining security and stability in that area “responds to the common interest of the international community”. China, Tehran's main trading partner and its biggest oil buyer, has repeatedly condemned attacks on Iran by the United States and Israel for “violating the sovereignty” of the Persian country.

item.title: José Simões Morais
item.description: The Jesuit Matteo Ricci (1552-1610) initially interpreted Chinese rites as superstitious, but to understand them better he studied the doctrine of Confucius, on which Chinese society and its political system were based with the principles of Order, Peace and Harmony. The name Confucius was given by Ricci to Kong Fu Zi （孔夫子， 551-479 BC), master Kong (Kongzi), who was born in the Spring-Autumn Period (770-476 BC), two thousand years ago. He lived during the Eastern Zhou Dynasty, when hundreds of small kingdoms that had previously been federated began to be conquered and great instability arose due to continuous wars between the increasingly larger and stronger kingdoms. Without qualified rulers for this new space of strategic management, many enlightened masters appeared, including Kongzi, who offered their knowledge, placing them at the service of the king for governance. Kongzi reorganized ancient knowledge and, being a great educator, transmitted it to his disciples. He instilled respect for the Ancestors, as a way of life he proposed the improvement of ethics through righteousness, benevolence and virtues. As a philosophy, the path of practical wisdom for harmony in social life, education and politics, attaching great importance to Peace, Harmony and Order as a principle of good governance and organization of the State. After his death in 479 BC, the teachings, despite having been marginalized at the time, had Confucian followers, excellent and powerful administrators. In 213 B.C.E. Most of the writings were destroyed by fire, but reproduced from memory they were perpetuated in Chinese society from the Han dynasty onwards (206 BC-220). Kongzi advocated the concept of Harmony: an ordered Universe, in which human beings must seek to maintain harmonious relationships with Nature, with themselves and with others. He regulated human relationships, for which he found five fundamental ones: sovereign and subjects, parents and children, husband and wife, older brother and younger brothers and between friends. "If the duties between one another are observed, there is harmony; otherwise, disharmony reigns. The most important relationship is that of father and son and the central virtue that governs it is filial piety (孝, xiao). On this virtue and those that accompany it – obedience, respect and service – the structure of Chinese society was built." Between quotation marks is what I believe to be by Father Benjamim Videira Pires, but it could have another author and for this mistake I apologize and thank anyone who corrects and dispels this doubt. The article from here onwards is structured around this text, from which the citations come, as I believe the subject deserves enough importance to be published. The kow-tow in rites "Filial piety should not cease with the death of parents; the child must continue to serve them as if they were alive, and this duty extends to all ancestors of the family. Confucius, by extending filial piety to the dead, fostered this virtue in life", taking actions to be carried out as a show of respect for ancestors and the desire to achieve their perfections with the help of example, imitating them; – the best way to honor family. In memory of the Ancestors, rituals were carried out in the houses where wooden tablets were found inscribed with the names of the deceased male ancestors, father, grandfather and great-grandfather, as when the great-great-grandfather became the tablet the right to appear in the Family Temple, until after a few generations it entered that of the Ancestors. Expressions of reverence and respect were addressed to the tablets, in an environment with flowers, candles, incense, as the intention is “to arrange a certain place or term to which they can elevate their minds; and to which, as to a painting, they can direct the customary honors, as if they were present.” In this ceremony, "kau-táu (headbanging) was performed, in which a person kneels and bows deeply until their forehead touches the ground. It is the Chinese custom of respect: nine prostrations to the throne at imperial receptions; prostrations in the courts before magistrates and of children before their parents and in-laws. Thus, there is nothing religious about the prostrations at funerals before the coffin and it could not be said that it was worship of the dead." All this led Ricci to modify the original opinion: "what to the European seemed like superstition and worship, to the Chinese it was just a symbolic expression of filial piety. The fact that they used the same ceremonies for the living led us to believe that they were not religious acts when applied to the dead. Such was the case with the burning of incense (…). Educated people used to welcome guests with incense, which was part of the ceremonial etiquette, which gave grace and harmony to social relations, being a sign of respect. There was therefore no reason to give it a religious meaning when it was used at funerals, before tablets or in the rooms of Confucius.” After long discussions with Chinese friends, Ricci concluded that there was nothing idolatrous in the ancestral rites or perhaps superstitious and "submitted these opinions to Valignano, who discussed them with his consultants. The conclusion was that it was permitted to participate in the solemn rites in honor of the family's ancestors on the condition that paper money was not burned, but it was not permitted to take part in the solemn ceremonies in honor of Confucius with the sacrifice of a victim, because, as he said Furtado, “smell strongly of superstition” (Quoted by Dunne, Generation of Giants).” On days prescribed by the ritual, with the appearance of religious sacrifices, an animal (immolated on site), wine, silk… was offered to the ancestors, following a family banquet. According to the Jesuits, “The idea of ​​spirits taking nourishment from the food offered should be repudiated; and of not making prayers or requests to the dead”. Other ceremonies the Jesuits permitted and even observed. “On the occasion of someone's death, the friends, who came to offer their condolences, would wear, in mourning, a white silk robe; upon entering the room, they would perform the kau-t'au four times in front of the deceased; the deceased's children would then repeat the same gesture; the visitor, approaching closer to the coffin, would perform the same ceremony again. The rites in honor of Confucius were only practiced by literate people and did not differ in essence from ancestral ceremonies,” and after “passing the exams, prostrations were made before Confucius’ tablet in a room dedicated to him. The same rites, prostrations and burning of incense were observed by officials and scholars on the days of the new moon and full moon.” Confucianism is based on the teachings of Kong Fuzi, a reformer who, using the rites of the Zhou, reorganized the order of society and structured human thought in China through the concepts of virtue, justice, tolerance and generosity. For practical wisdom, the importance of Astronomy and the preparation of the Calendar, as any inaccuracy disturbs the balance between Heaven and Earth. 完

item.title: Iran | War breaks out in the Middle East with attacks that killed Khamenei
item.description:
The United States and Israel launched a series of attacks against Iran. One of the fatal victims was Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as well as several Iranian military leaders. The Shah's heir, Reza Pahlavi, believes in a bright future, at a time when Tehran's counterattacks are spreading across the Middle East
Israel and the United States of America (USA) joined together in a joint operation to overthrow the Iranian regime, at a time when, in the country, there were already some demonstrations against the authorities. On Saturday, a series of bombings were launched against Iran, with the declared aim of forcing a change of regime. The country responded with attacks on Israel and US military bases, and the death of Iran's supreme religious leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who had been in power for 36 years, was also confirmed. According to Lusa, the announcement was made on Iranian state television at 5am local time, with a reporter crying over what had happened. Khameinei was 86 years old and, for Trump, his death now offers the Iranian population a “greater chance” of “recovering” the country. The Iranian regime declared 40 days of mourning for the death of the religious leader. In addition to Khamenei, the chief of staff of the Iranian Armed Forces, Abdolrahim Mousavi, was also killed, along with other high-ranking generals during the US and Israeli attacks against the country, Iranian state television reported yesterday. Meanwhile, China expressed “great concern” about the situation in the Middle East and called for respect for Iran's sovereignty and territorial integrity. “Iran's sovereignty, security and territorial integrity must be respected,” said the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs in a statement cited by the North American news agency The Associated Press (AP). Beijing called for “an immediate cessation of military actions and that there is no further escalation of a tense situation”. He also called for the “resumption of dialogue and negotiation” and efforts to “maintain peace and stability in the Middle East”, according to the statement also cited by the Spanish news agency Europa Press (EP). China has recommended the departure of its citizens from Iran, advising its citizens not to travel to the region, due to the “marked increase in security risks”, as the country faces repeated threats of attacks from the US. “Chinese citizens currently in Iran should reinforce their security precautions and leave the country as soon as possible,” the Consular Affairs Department of the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs said in a statement. A nuclear issue North American President Donald Trump has threatened to attack Iran due to the authorities' repression of protests against the regime and alleging that Tehran is developing nuclear weapons, despite having attacked sites on Iranian soil in June precisely with the aim of stopping the alleged nuclear program. At the end of the campaign, Trump claimed that Iran's nuclear program had been completely obliterated. On Tuesday - and despite talks in Geneva about an agreement regarding Iran's nuclear program - Trump once again said, during his State of the Nation address in Congress, that he will not allow Iran to have nuclear weapons. In 2015, Iran signed an agreement to limit its nuclear development – ​​in exchange for the relief of economic sanctions on the country – with the five permanent members of the UN Security Council — the United States, Russia, China, France and the United Kingdom, as well as with Germany and the European Union. However, the agreement lost effect after Trump himself unilaterally withdrew the United States from the treaty in 2018, during his first term (2017-2021). China's recommendations join those of several other countries that have raised their security alerts, such as Sweden, Serbia, Germany, India, South Korea, Brazil, Australia, the United Kingdom or Canada. Tension in the Middle East Ali Khamenei's death puts the future of the Islamic Republic in doubt and increases the risk of regional instability. “Khamenei, one of the most evil people in history, is dead,” Trump wrote in a social media post, warning of the continuation of “heavy and precise bombings” throughout the week and even beyond. Saturday's attack opened a new chapter in the US intervention in Iran, bringing with it the potential for retaliatory violence and a broader war. The truth is that there are already pockets of tension thanks to the Iranian response, with attacks on around ten countries, including the United Arab Emirates. In Dubai, a strong explosion occurred in a luxury hotel area, causing four injuries, while Dubai International Airport was also the target of an attack, leading to the cancellation of all flights. Another source of tension occurred in Karachi, Pakistan, resulting in the death of six people and 20 injuries during a protest near the fortified area of ​​the United States Consulate. According to local police spokesman Rehan Ali, to CNN, “hundreds of people suddenly appeared in the vicinity” of the diplomatic mission, leading to a rapid mobilization of security forces. The screams captured on the recordings suggest that the protest was motivated by attacks involving the United States and Israel against neighboring Iran. A country without a leader Khamenei's death in the Trump Administration's second attack on Iran in eight months creates a leadership vacuum, given the absence of a known ayatollah successor, and also because the 86-year-old supreme leader had the final say on all major policies during decades in power. Khamenei led Iran's clerical establishment and its paramilitary Revolutionary Guard, the two main centers of power in the ruling theocracy. Ali Larijani, secretary of Iran's National Security Council, said on Saturday that Israel and the United States "will regret their actions." “The brave soldiers and the great nation of Iran will teach an unforgettable lesson to the infernal international oppressors,” Larijani posted on the X social network. The joint operation by the United States and Israel, which according to authorities was planned for months, took place this Saturday, during the Muslim holy month of Ramadan and at the beginning of the Iranian work week, and followed negotiations and warnings from Trump, who last year trumpeted his government's success in crippling the country's nuclear program. About 12 hours after the attacks began, the U.S. military reported that there were no American casualties and that damage to U.S. bases was minimal despite “hundreds of Iranian missile and drone strikes.” According to US forces, targets in Iran included Revolutionary Guard command facilities, air defense systems, missile and drone launch sites and military airfields. Regarding Khamenei's death, Trump declared that the ayatollah “was unable to escape highly sophisticated intelligence and tracking systems”. “Working closely with Israel, there was nothing he, or the other leaders who were killed with him, could do,” said the American President. “This is the greatest opportunity for the Iranian people to take back their country,” he said. An Iranian diplomat told the United Nations Security Council that hundreds of civilians were killed and injured in the attacks. Iran retaliated by firing missiles and drones at Israel and US military bases in the region, and fighting continued into the night. US Democrats criticized the fact that Trump took measures without authorization from Congress, but White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said that the Administration informed several Republican and Democratic leaders in Congress in advance. What does the Shah's son say At a time when regime change appears to be imminent in Iran, but with no clear understanding of how it will happen, one of the voices that has defended US and Israeli action is Reza Pahlavi, son of the former Shah. Having lived in the USA for decades, since the Iranian revolution of 1979, Pahlavi is now arguing that it is time to take to the streets. According to Lusa, the heir stated, in an editorial published in The Washington Post, that he intends to take on a role in the transition process in Iran. "Many Iranians, often despite bullets, asked me to lead this transition. I am impressed by your courage and responded to your call", he wrote. "Our path forward will be transparent: a new Constitution written and ratified by referendum, followed by free elections under international supervision. When Iranians vote, the transitional government will be dissolved." For the past 47 years, Pahlavi has been a critical voice of the Iranian regime, now siding with Trump and Israel. Domestically and regionally, Pahlavi is widely regarded as a puppet of Washington and Tel Aviv. In the article, he praises the entire trajectory that Trump has taken towards Iran, saying that the country's “rebirth” “will mark the beginning of an era of peace and prosperity”. Pahlavi also highlighted that “Iran is not Iraq”. "We will not repeat the mistakes that followed this [Iraq] conflict. There will be no dissolution of institutions, no power vacuum, no chaos", he said, recalling that since last year he has been defending the so-called "Iran Prosperity Project". It is a national political and economic plan to establish a transitional provisional government after the fall of the ayatollahs. The Iranian activist guarantees that “this detailed roadmap for national recovery” includes a plan for “the first 100 days after the collapse of the regime and the long-term reconstruction and stabilization” of the country and “has the support of numerous business leaders from around the world”. “A democratic Iran would transform the Middle East, converting one of the most persistent sources of unrest in the world into a pillar of regional stability”, says Pahlavi, who praises the “historic” Abraham agreements, promoted by Trump in his first term.

item.title: Crime | Study reveals perception of increase, even with downward trend
item.description: Although official data reveals fewer crimes in the Macao SAR, this reality is not reflected in society's perception. A study reveals that in Macau only 13 percent of respondents were aware of the drop in crime and that more than 35 percent believed that crime had increased The study “Examining fear of crime among Macao residents: Vulnerability, victimization and situational factors”, published in the journal International Review of Victimology, reveals that in the territory the population does not fear crime, although perceptions regarding crime are not always in line with official data. The work, authored by Yixuan Wang, Donna Soi Wan Leong and Lichao Lu, from the University of Macau (UM) and Jianhong Liu, from the Macau University of Science and Technology (MUST), reveals that there was, between 2021 and 2022, a “13.9 percent decline in crime”, according to data released by the Security Services Directorate in 2023. However, “the survey data from this study reveals a significant discrepancy in perception: only 12.83 percent of respondents reported being aware of the decline, while 35.72 percent believed that crime had increased,” it reads. Furthermore, “the vast majority of respondents did not demonstrate emotional fear” in the face of crime, as “78.87 percent reported zero unsafe places and only 2.77 percent identified two or more” unsafe areas in Macau. Meanwhile, “only 9.69 percent of respondents reported feeling at risk of victimization, indicating that the vast majority did not feel at risk”. The study also shows that “the proportion of residents who reported defensive behavioral responses was the lowest among the fear dimensions, with only 2.89 percent of respondents indicating that they had adopted defensive behaviors in the previous 12 months”. The authors describe how Macau constitutes a paradigmatic example for analyzing social fear of crime and perceptions regarding official data, as it is a special administrative region that “functions under the ‘one country, two systems’ governance framework, characterized by a distinct sociopolitical and legal structure, along with a multicultural population”. Furthermore, it is mentioned that Macau has an economy “that depends exclusively on gambling and tourism, which drives rapid financial circulation and large-scale population mobility”, being a “small urban territory with an exceptionally high population density”. In this way, the particular characteristics of the territory “raise questions about the applicability of existing models of fear of crime, since residents' perceptions of crime and disorder can be shaped by dense urban environments and rapid population flows”. A question of age The study was carried out based on the 2022 Macau Victimization Survey, managed by the Macau Society of Criminology, a project funded by the Macau Foundation. The target population of the survey were residents over 18 years of age, and for this study, additional telephone interviews were carried out with 1,102 people, resulting in a final sample of 795 people. This study also states that “the results reveal consistencies and deviations in relation to previous research”, as “contrary to what has been established, age and gender do not significantly influence the fear of crime”. For the authors, the “unexpected result” can be explained by “institutional and Macau-specific factors”, as the “Macau Government has placed great emphasis on investment in social well-being, which can act as a buffer against fear for potentially vulnerable groups”. The measure of financial support for the repair of buildings is highlighted, such as the Building Maintenance Support Scheme and the Subsidy Scheme for Maintenance of Common Parts, which “further reduces the need for residents to implement additional defensive strategies at the domestic level”. The study highlights the “extensive welfare infrastructure in Macau”, such as “universal healthcare, old-age pensions and social protection schemes, which can mitigate perceived vulnerability among the elderly”. Laws that help Regarding gender-based violence, it is described how the Domestic Violence Law and “strengthened support systems for victims can contribute to a feeling of security associated with gender”. In 2022, there were 22 cases of rape and eight of sexual coercion, according to data from the Institute of Social Action (IAS) cited in the study. The authors also highlight that Macau's Gender Inequality Index was 0.06 in 2021, “substantially lower” than that of mainland China (0.192), Japan (0.083) and the global average (0.457). This indicates “a relatively equitable environment in terms of gender”, according to mentions made by the IAS in 2021 and the United Nations Development Program, in 2022. The academics describe how “these factors, taken together, may mitigate the relevance of physical vulnerability in the formation of fear of crime, explaining the lack of significant effects of age and gender”. The study highlights that the fact that “consistently low levels of fear” regarding crime can be “partially attributed to the Macau Government's substantial investment in public security and the high density of police resources”. It appears, therefore, that “the strong policing and investment in public security in Macau”, even with the low crime rate, results “in an equally limited prevalence of victimization in the sample”. Furthermore, "the relatively low levels of fear reported by Macau residents can be explained by both substantive cultural mechanisms and methodological factors. At the substantive level, collectivist and relational cultural orientations can exert a genuinely buffering effect", since in Chinese societies "individuals are embedded in dense family, kinship and community networks", which can facilitate "access to social support and mitigate stress and fear responses", the study reads. Signals to the Executive The academics argue that these results “present several implications for social governance in Macau”, given the existence of a “paradox between residents’ subjective perceptions of crime and the decline reflected in official statistics”. All of this “underlines the importance of reinforcing transparency in the dissemination of information and law enforcement”, with “crime-related information being disseminated through multiple channels and improving the visibility of prevention efforts”, which can “contribute to reducing fear and strengthening public trust”. The Government's actions in the area of ​​security seem to produce some results, the study describes, which is “particularly relevant in the context of rapid population aging”. “In addition to maintaining these investments, strengthening informal social control can also contribute to increasing the collective effectiveness of communities. Initiatives such as the expansion of volunteering programs and support for neighborhood associations (e.g. Federation of Residents' Associations of Macau; Association of Youth Volunteers of Macau) can provide additional social resources to mitigate risks associated with physical vulnerability”, it is argued.

item.title: UN | Human rights under large-scale attack
item.description: Human rights “are under attack on a large scale throughout the world”, carried out “by those who hold the greatest power” and, sometimes, even “with pride”, denounced the Secretary-General of the United Nations yesterday. "The rule of law is being replaced by the rule of force. And this attack does not come from the shadows nor is it a surprise. It is happening in plain sight and, often, led by those who hold the greatest power. All over the world, human rights are being deliberately and strategically repressed, and sometimes with pride", declared Guterres, speaking in Geneva at the opening session of the 61st session of the UN Human Rights Council. The secretary-general warned that the consequences of this attack “are devastating”, pointing out that people are suffering “twice: first with violence, oppression or exclusion, and then again with the world’s indifference”. "We live in a world where mass suffering is ignored, where human beings are used as bargaining chips, where international law is treated as a mere inconvenience. Conflicts multiply and impunity has become contagious. This is not due to a lack of knowledge, tools or institutions. It is the result of political choices", deplored the former Portuguese Prime Minister. According to António Guterres, “this crisis of respect for human rights is not an isolated case” and “reflects and magnifies all other global fractures”, as “humanitarian needs are exploding, while financing is collapsing”, “inequalities are increasing at an impressive speed, countries are drowning in debt and despair, and climate chaos is accelerating”. The United Nations leader also highlighted that “technology, especially artificial intelligence, is increasingly being used in ways that suppress rights, deepen inequality and expose marginalized people to new forms of discrimination, both online and offline”. "On all fronts, those who are already vulnerable are being pushed further to the margins. And human rights defenders are among the first to be silenced when they try to warn us", he lamented, reinforcing that, "in this coordinated offensive, human rights are the first victims". Deplorable portrait Exposing a devastating portrait at a global level, the UN Secretary-General pointed out that today an “increasing tightening of civic space” is evident, with “journalists and activists imprisoned, NGOs [Non-Governmental Organizations] closed, women's rights in retreat, children's rights ignored, people with disabilities excluded and democracies in erosion”. "The right to peaceful assembly is crushed - and I once again condemn the recent violent crackdown on protests in Iran. Migrants are persecuted, detained and expelled, with complete disregard for their human rights and humanity. Refugees are made into scapegoats. LGBTIQ+ communities are vilified. Minorities and indigenous peoples are targeted. Religious communities are attacked," he continued. Alluding to his personal history, the last time he spoke as secretary-general at the opening of a session of the Human Rights Council, he pointed out that “growing up under the Salazar dictatorship” taught him that “the denial of human rights corrodes all aspects of society. “When the Security Council is paralyzed, when vetoes serve as a political shield, when geopolitical rivalries prevail over the protection of civilians, the result is the same: impunity spreads, suffering multiplies and human rights are trampled on,” he said. “We need a Security Council that reflects the world of today, not the world of 1945,” he added.

item.title: Culture | There is a lack of public support to build a book market
item.description: Small market, little sales outside the territory and lack of support: book publishing in Macau faces problems in solidifying itself and competing with what is done abroad. Representative Lam Fat Iam recently defended policies to support the construction of a “Reading City”. Pin-to Livros & Música and the publisher Mandarina point out gaps that impede the dynamism of the sector It was in February, in an intervention before the agenda, that deputy Lam Fat Iam launched the challenge in the Legislative Assembly (AL): “support the development of local physical bookstores and build a ‘Reading City'”.
