The ability to transform herself has earned Kiri T a reputation as an artist with expanding artistic vision and expressiveness. “Fluent in Cantonese, English and Mandarin, the singer transitions easily between languages, combining refined vocal control with emotional sincerity to create songs that resonate deeply”, describes the organization. Despite the creative frenzy, Kiri T created an introverted persona focused on writing songs that resemble emotional diaries, with quiet and honest reflections on love and everyday life, offering moments of recognition and comfort in whispered ballads.
The kindness of millions A popularity phenomenon, Kiri T has accumulated millions of streams on platforms such as Spotify or YouTube. For example, the music video for the song “You Gotta Screw Up At Least Once”, released around two years ago, has already surpassed 11 million views on YouTube and 15 million streams on Spotify. On her Spotify profile, Kiri T states that she grew up between Hong Kong, the United Kingdom and the United States. "I love beautiful melodies, lush instrumentation and lyrics that cut deep. I suffer from insane anxiety, but puppies make everything better." This is how the singer describes herself. Kiri T's career started very early. At the age of 14, he was already composing songs for Cantopop diva Joey Yung. Before reaching the age of 20, and with some songs written, Kiri T signed with Denise Ho's Goomusic label. In 2015, he released his first single under his own name “Twenty-Something”, which ended up being remixed by Lucian, surpassing 7 million streams on Spotify. This exhibition lit the spark that exploded the young artist’s career. Among singles released in English or Cantonese, in 2019 Kiri T's debut album “Golden Kiri” was released, the first of three albums in two years. In 2022, the singer won the awards for best singer and best song at the Whats Good Music Awards, with the recognition being extended to other music awards. João Luz

item.title: Exhibition | Watercolors by Hana Tou Mei Kun at Fantasia 10 until April 29
item.description: The exhibition “Watercolors of Hana Tou Mei Kun” is on display in the Fantasia building, on Calçada da Igreja de São Lázaro, until April 29th. The exhibition brings together more than 30 recent pieces by the young local artist, “with emphasis on watercolor landscapes, along with sketches and works in acrylic and tile paintings”. According to curator Leong Kit Man, Hana Tou Mei Kun's paintings “capture the serene beauty of a gentle breeze and, like the painting, so is the painter”. The exhibition curator describes the artist as “an empathetic, kind and caring art teacher who cultivated countless young artistic talents”. Hana Tou Mei Kun was a student of local artist Dor Lio Hak Man and continued her studies in Taiwan, at National Chiayi University and Ming Chuan University. Currently, she is an art teacher Reflecting on her years of practice, the artist reveals that “she has always felt attracted to watercolor landscapes, with the quick and free flow of brushstrokes inspiring her great joy”, describes the organization of the exhibition. “Watercolors of Hana Tou Mei Kun” is organized by the Macau Young Art Association, in collaboration with the Promotion Association for Creative Industries in the Parish of São Lázaro, 10 Fantasia and the Macau Art Teachers Communication Association. The exhibition can be visited daily, between 11am and 6pm, except on Mondays.

item.title: Cinema | Applications for support for short films are open until April 10th
item.description: The application period for the financial support program for short film productions is open until April 10th, aimed at residents with ID cards over 18 years of age. The program has three categories: Documentary, Fiction Short Film and Animation, and each category is divided into three levels: Advanced, Free and Beginners. The program's jury will be made up of professionals from the sector, invited by the Cultural Institute, who will select a maximum of 16 projects, which will receive a “total amount of up to 1.55 million patacas, depending on the category and level”. Each selected project may receive, individually, a maximum value of 280 thousand patacas for production costs. Among the short films selected, the jury will select a maximum of three outstanding works, whose participants will be entitled to strategic planning services for film festivals and international commercial promotion, for one year. Interested parties can consult the regulations and download the registration form on the Cultural Institute portal. The forms and appropriate documentation must be delivered to the Cultural Institute Building, in Praça do Tap Siac, by 5:30 pm on April 10th, or via email by 11:59 pm on the same day. The Government emphasizes that the granting of this support aims to "increase professionalism, market competitiveness and international visibility of selected short films. This year the program introduces new specific support measures".

item.title: Chinese culture | Workshops bring forward the end of the show season
item.description:
The end of the 1st Season of Chinese Culture Shows is approaching. At the end of March, the dance theater play “Mulan” will be presented, and this weekend a series of workshops inspired by the mythological character will begin. Clothing, crafts and jewelry will be the themes of the workshops that will take place at the Macau Literature House and the Lou Kau House
Starting on Saturday, the Macau House of Literature and the House of Lou Kau will host three workshops inspired by the ancient legend of Mulan, in anticipation of the shows that will close the 1st Season of Chinese Culture Shows. The “Dress and Dance like Mulan” Workshop will provide a shared experience between parents and children, aged between 4 and 8, dressed in ethnic Chinese dance costumes. This activity takes place at the Macau Literature House on Saturday, between 10:30 am and 12:30 pm and from 3:00 pm to 5:00 pm, and on Sunday, from 3:00 pm to 5:00 pm. Casa de Lou Kau will host two workshops, one on quilling (drawings made with rolled paper, satin ribbons and other materials) and modeling in light clay, aimed at participants aged 6 and over. “Mulan: Paper Filigree Workshop” is scheduled for the 14th, 21st and 28th of March, always between 3pm and 5pm, while the “Mulan: Super-light Clay Creation Workshop” will take place on the 15th, 22nd and 29th of March, at the same time. The Cultural Institute (IC) indicates that interested Macau residents can check the details and register through the “Activities Registration” section of the Macau Single Account. If the number of registrants exceeds capacity, the selection of participants will be done by draw. The beginning of the end The 1st Season of Chinese Culture Shows ends with the dance theater piece “Mulan”, by the Ningbo Arts and Entertainment Group, which will take the stage at the Grand Auditorium of the Macau Cultural Center on the 27th and 28th of March, at 7:30 pm. The piece, jointly directed by the “acclaimed twin stars of Chinese dance” Zhou Liya and Han Zhen, won several national awards, including the “Five-One Project Award” and the “Chinese Dance Lotus Flower Award”, with performances in various countries and regions, both in China and abroad. Tickets for shows at the Macau Cultural Center are on sale at the Enjoy Macao Ticket Office, and cost between 120 and 400 patacas, with a 50 percent discount for those who have a “valid Macau Resident Identity Card, Full-Time Student Card, Macau Teacher Card”, highlights the IC. Mulan is a classic legend well known in China. Centered on filial piety, loyalty, courage and love, the work narrates the journey and transformation of a beautiful young peasant girl into a legendary heroine, “transmitting traditional Chinese virtues and humanist feelings that transcend time and space”. “By ingeniously fusing dance, martial arts, theater and technological elements, the production gives renewed vitality to classic characters”, describes the IC. The play centered on the mythological figure of the peasant woman turned heroic warrior closes with a flourish the first season of shows that started in November last year. In total, 37 shows and parallel activities were organized, which the IC indicates were “seen by more than ten thousand people”. The cultural cycle is sponsored by the Secretariat for Social Affairs and Culture and is jointly organized by the IC and the Department of Advertising, Culture and Sports of the Liaison Office of the Central People's Government in the Macao SAR. Furthermore, it also has the support of the Tourism and Education and Youth Development departments and the Institute for Municipal Affairs.

item.title: Economy | Request return of consumption coupons
item.description:
The Macau Industrial and Commercial Association defends a hybrid economy, with periods in which SMEs live based on an increase in tourists, in high seasons, and support for consumption, during low seasons.
Given the reduction in the number of tourists in Macau, post-Lunar New Year, the executive president of the Macau Industrial and Commercial Association, Ng Wah Wai, defended more measures to encourage domestic consumption, such as discount coupons. Ng's position was shared in statements cited by the Ou Mun newspaper. According to the Macau Industrial and Commercial Association, discount coupons are an efficient way to promote the economy during periods when there are fewer tourists in the territory. Ng defended a hybrid economy, with periods in which Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) can survive solely on additional income from tourists, during high seasons, and others in which, faced with the reduction in visitors, SMEs receive help from consumption coupons. Ng Wah Wai also warned that the number of visitors to Macau will continue to gradually decrease in the coming months, so the distribution of coupons is vital to boost the community economy, and alleviate the concerns of SMEs. The executive president of the Macau Industrial and Commercial Association also called on the Executive to consider allowing the use of coupons in more sectors, in addition to restaurants and retail, and to increase the amount directly allocated to the elderly. New models The possibility of distributing more consumer coupons was also discussed by Matthew Liu Ting Chi, professor at the Faculty of Business Management at the University of Macau. Liu defended the coupon program, considering that from a macroeconomic and microeconomic perspective these measures to encourage consumption effectively bring new vitality to the market. However, the academic suggested that the Executive consider changing the model used in recent editions of allocating coupons. Until now, this type of program has worked with coupons that can be obtained through a draw, based on consumption on weekdays. The coupons can then be used on the weekend immediately following the draw, and only on those days. Liu Ting Chi suggests a new model in which people can only qualify for coupons when they consume on the weekend, and can then use the discounts during weekdays. The academic also asked for a longer period for the use of coupons, beyond the weekend immediately following the award, to allow for new consumption patterns. In this way, the UM academic believes that consumption will cover more sectors, in addition to supermarkets, pharmacies and restaurants, the sectors most benefited by previous measures.

item.title: IAM | Portuguese sidewalks can be made of non-slip material
item.description:
Portuguese sidewalks outside historic areas can be replaced with non-slip material, maintaining the original pattern and design, according to a person responsible for the Institute for Municipal Affairs. In historic areas, authorities will maintain the sidewalk, one of the city's distinctive elements
The Portuguese sidewalk can be replaced with another type of flooring, non-slip, but maintaining the same type of pattern and aesthetics. The idea was put forward yesterday by Ng Chi Lun, from the Department of Public Roads and Sanitation of the Institute for Municipal Affairs (IAM), during the Forum Macau program on the Chinese channel Rádio Macau. The official stated that his department will continue to inspect and optimize Portuguese sidewalks in the city's historic areas, as well as in buffer zones, adjacent to areas subject to heritage protection. The main theme of the day was the city's cleaning campaign, which will advance at the end of the month. According to IAM president Chao Wai Ieng, this year's campaign will be more comprehensive than last year's, and will last longer. "We launched the campaign for the first time last year, with the participation of various public services, associations and institutions, totaling around 50 thousand people. We set up consultation and exhibition points in residential neighborhoods and raised the population's awareness of maintaining hygienic and clean urban environments, and combating rats", said the president of IAM. Reach further Chao Wai Ieng also stated that this year the IAM intends to increase the frequency of cleaning actions in buildings without a condominium assembly, residents' organization or a company that takes care of the administration. Last year, garbage was the cause of the vast majority of complaints received by the City Beautification and Cleaning Working Group, which followed up on 7,489 complaints regarding the hygiene and beauty of the streets. Of these nearly 7,500 cases, about 97 percent have been resolved. Also last year, the IAM punished around 15 thousand infractions for hygienic reasons, more than half related to the accumulation of rubbish on public roads. As for offenses committed by tourists, the most frequent were throwing rubbish on the ground or spitting. The head of the IAM's Environmental Hygiene and Licensing Department, in response to a listener, stated that Taipa Central Park stands out as one of the places targeted for the most complaints due to the presence of dog feces.

item.title: Technology | Woman excited by robot received medical assistance
item.description:
The CPSP reported that the humanoid robot was controlled by a man aged around 50, who aims to commercialize this type of equipment in the future
The Public Security Police Corps (CPSP) announced that the woman who was seen arguing with a humanoid robot had to receive medical treatment after being frightened. The information was provided in a response sent to HM. "Around 9pm on March 5th, the Public Security Police received a report that a woman needed help outside a building on Rua do Sul, in S. Lázaro. The agents immediately went to the location", it was indicated. "It was found that the woman, while using her cell phone, was startled when she realized that a robot was approaching from behind. There was no physical contact between the woman and the robot, nor did she suffer any injuries. However, she reported feeling unwell and required hospital treatment", it was added. On Monday night, the woman had already been discharged, but chose not to file any type of complaint against the robot's owner. “She has been discharged and has not taken any legal action in relation to the incident,” it was reported. The CPSP also revealed that the robot was returned to the owner, after being advised to use it carefully. The same source clarified that the robot was not operated by artificial intelligence: “The robot involved was operated by a local man in his fifties, who stated that he was carrying out tests at the time, with the intention of using it for commercial promotions in the future”, it was pointed out. “Police reminded the man that when operating robots, he must remain constantly aware of the situation to avoid causing danger or alarm to the surrounding environment or passersby,” it added. Regulation requests This was the second public incident with a humanoid robot in the territory in recent weeks. Also in the first case, the robot was being tested and then commercialized, according to Zeng Zengwei, member of the North Zone Community Services Advisory Council. At the most recent meeting of this council, this member made a request to the authorities to implement new regulations to deal with robots, mainly given the possibility of them being controlled by artificial intelligence and due to image and sound capture equipment, which could jeopardize the protection of personal data.

item.title: Paradise Entertainment | Alert for reduced profits
item.description: Paradise Entertainment, which managed the Kam Pek satellite casino and provides gaming machines, warned investors of a 63.5 percent profit reduction in 2025 to HK$139.4 million. The warning was left in a statement sent to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, which states that part of the reduction in profits was due to payments made to SJM related to workers at the Kam Pek satellite casino, which closed last year. “This decrease in profits was mainly due […] to the group's payments to employees and reimbursements to SJM Resorts, related to employers contracted by SJM Resorts who worked for Kam Pek Paradise Casino,” it was stated. These payments totaled 42.1 million Hong Kong dollars. Among the factors that contributed to the reduction in profits, the company also highlighted a decrease of 85.2 million Hong Kong dollars in revenue from the provision of casino management services in Macau and a reduction of 165.5 million Hong Kong dollars in the sale of gaming equipment. In 2024, the company responsible for operating Kam Pek had recorded profits of 245.5 million Hong Kong dollars.

item.title: Game | Citigroup highlights “solid start” to March
item.description: March got off to a “solid start” in terms of gaming revenue, according to the latest report from investment bank Citigroup. The document is cited by the GGRAsia portal. “Based on our industry sources, we found that Macau’s gross gaming revenues in the first eight days of March reached around MOP5.65 billion,” stated in the report signed by George Choi and Timothy Chau. “This implies an average daily revenue of around 706 million patacas, around 4 percent lower than the measure of gross gaming revenue in February 2026 – 737 million patacas per day –, but around 11 percent higher than that of March 2025, of 634 million patacas per day”, it was added. According to Citi, the various shows organized by casino concessionaires at the beginning of March contributed to the annual increase in gaming revenue. “We believe that the concerts by Korean boy band Treasure at the Venetian Arena, Chinese singer-songwriter Wang Sulong and Korean girl group Aespa at Arena Galaxy may have contributed to the solid start to March 2026”, it was pointed out. The good numbers still represent an expected reduction in revenue compared to February, which is normally one of the game's peak seasons, as it coincides with the Lunar New Year. “Based on industry sources, VIP volumes fell between 2 percent and 4 percent compared to the previous month, and mass revenues fell between 3 percent and 5 percent compared to the previous month,” they noted.

item.title: Hutchison | Sale of “3” in Macau yielded 110 million
item.description: The sale of “3” was explained by the losses of the operator linked to Hutchison Telecom, which reached 43 million Hong Kong dollars. Macau's telecommunications market is now entirely concentrated in Chinese state-owned companies CITIC and China Telecom The sale of telecommunications operator “3” by Hutchison Telecom to Macau Telecommunications Company (CTM) netted the Hong Kong company 110 million Hong Kong dollars. The numbers appear in the company's annual results, released in a statement to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on Monday. “In January 2026, the Group completed the sale of all its interests in 3 Macau for a value of 110 million Hong Kong dollars, ending more than two decades of operations in the Macau market”, was explained by Canning Fok Kin Ning, president of Hutchison Telecom, which is part of the group founded by Li Ka-shing. “The sale will serve to support sustained improvements in the group’s profitability in the coming years, allowing it to redistribute resources more effectively and further simplify its operational structure,” added the president. The document also shows that the company's losses in Macau had been increasing in recent years. In 2025, losses in Macau were 43 million Hong Kong dollars, a figure that increased 72 percent compared to the previous year, when losses in Macau were 25 million Hong Kong dollars. This is a difference of 18 million Hong Kong dollars, which had a significant impact on the group's results. The year 2025 closed for Hutchison Telecom with losses of 25 million Hong Kong dollars, when the previous year it had recorded a profit of 6 million. Monopoly regime With the sale of Hutchison to CTM, the market is formally left with three operators, as the 3 brand will be maintained for now, but CTM and 3 will be controlled by the same shareholders, essentially the CITIC Group, a Chinese state-owned company. The other operator in the Macau market is also a Chinese state-owned company, in this case China Telecom. This situation has raised concerns about a possible weakening of the clients' position, as was exposed by the association Centro da Políticas da Sabedoria Colectiva, through the vice-president of Loi Man Keong. However, the Government dismissed any concerns, devaluing the concentration and promised to continue carrying out regularization functions. “This transfer of shareholdings essentially involves a change in the shareholders of Hutchison, which continues to operate its network and provide services”, was indicated in a statement from the Postal and Telecommunications Services Department (CTT). “In other words, currently, the market continues to have three mobile telecommunications operators, and the Government continues to strictly monitor Hutchison's compliance with its license obligations, ensuring the provision of stable mobile telecommunications services to users”, it was added.

item.title: Questioned qualification of person responsible for restoration of the Ruins of São Paulo
item.description: The professional qualifications of one of those responsible for the restoration of the bronze statues in the Ruins of St. Paul, whose work began in 2017, was called into question in an article published by the newspaper Cheng Pou. The person in question is Ip Kin Hong, research assistant professor at the University of Macau. In October last year, comments from a cultural heritage restoration professional were published in the Chinese press, pointing out that the position of the statue of the Virgin Mary on the third level had been changed following the restoration. In response to the doubts, which gained some traction online, the Cultural Institute (IC) highlighted that it strictly respects international requirements regarding the protection and restoration of cultural heritage, following the principles of minimum and reversible intervention. Furthermore, he highlighted that the restoration of the city's landmarks has the support of the Forbidden City Museum and the University of Macau (UM), the Center for the Preservation and Transmission of Cultural Heritage of the Macau Palace Museum, subordinate to the IC. Cheng Pou also cites allegations that Ip Kin Hong has professional ties with the Center for the Preservation and Transmission of Cultural Heritage of the Macau Palace Museum and UM, the first entity responsible for the work, while the educational establishment was one of the entities consulted in relation to the quality of the restoration. As such, he questioned the IC about the criteria for deciding the entities responsible for the restoration work and the professional qualifications of the UM professor, the costs of the project, whether the team that handled the previous phases is in charge of the last phase of the work. In the article published yesterday, the newspaper states that until Monday it had no response from the IC. By phases The IC announced in the middle of last month that the second phase of the restoration and maintenance work on the bronze statues in the Ruins of St. Paul had been successfully completed. The first phase, completed last year, focused on the statue of the Virgin Mary on the third level and the two bronze statues to the right of the second level. The second phase, which mainly involved the two bronze statues on the left of the second level and the statue of Jesus Christ on the fourth level. The next and final phase will be the restoration this year of the Dove statue, located on the upper level.

item.title: ZELENSKY VS ZELENSKY
item.description:
10/27/22 A Ukrainian pensioner, with the same name as the president, blames Zelensky for the prolongation of the conflict that destroyed his village. Zelensky, the villager, who never left his land, is now mortified because his favorite gravedigger died in the bomber. This Zelensky, a villager, represents half the world that recriminates the other Zelensky, the villain, for not wanting to negotiate with Putin. Negotiate what? The invader enters our house, covers his wife and daughter (cf. recent UN report on the rapes in Ukraine, in this “special operation”), an act that forced us to watch, and then we say to him: hi there, we are for peace, but we have to negotiate: you still haven't said what name you want to give the children! A peace that is not dignified is nothing more than a cessation of rights. As serious as the Russophobia that Putin inflicts on his people.
10/28/22 At a lunch with several guests, I heard a friend, who declared herself a spiritualist, explaining the particularities of her belief, in a calm and balanced way, I would even say, without a hint of “irrationalism”. He spoke long and serenely, devoid of any fanatical proselytism. “Here is someone whose belief has brought thoughtfulness!” I thought, a rare alliance. However, at a certain moment a sentence comes out that horrifies me. He says, “there is no chance, nothing happens by chance!”, and I glimpse the voracious maws of holism. Because holism has a pathological aspect, like all good things, a shadow side. Two examples: in Hinduism there is less sensitivity to existential dramas, if that child suffers in this life this is just an expression of rigorous karmic symmetry, an effect of the acts he committed in his previous life. In Africa, it is not accepted that death could have been accidental, and many women are accused by the family of the deceased of having caused the death of their husband, in an act of manifest dementia, and, in the blatant majority of cases, a huge injustice for the wife — madness that spreads and infiltrates the social fabric. Buddhism introduced Compassion and Christianity introduced Caritas, trying to break with this logic, but in many other beliefs and religions the cage of causalities prevails over sensitivity to experience, the need to respond to the now. It is a tremendous logic that transforms the world into a Platonic stage, where we are nothing more than the shadows of something — an inextricable law -, in a determinism that calcifies all singularities, but that strangely fascinates many. The conquest of modernity was anchored in the conquest of chance and randomness, removing the incidences of a life from this fierce determinism that enclosed the world in a web. When the Renaissance freed the body from the influence of the stars, at that moment sexuality was relieved of guilt and individualism and its volition were able to emerge. There is an unequivocal moral implication in the phrase “there is no such thing as chance, nothing happens by chance!”, which should function as a key to self-knowledge and self-judgment, however, this phrase is usually used to seek a reason for things outside of us, in others. It's an alibi. Today there is a holistic temptation in the world, just as there was a satanic temptation in the Middle Ages, without much reflection on its consequences. Because it's not enough to want to connect everything in a resonant chain of affection. Even for the most unexpected: affection itself can kill. I realize, thanks to that cultured, balanced, intelligent woman, that “the fight” took place at the beginning of this century. One of the great mistakes of the 20th century was the false dichotomy between “rationalism and irrationalism”, a debate that extended to all fields, including art. First, rationality (the positive side of reason) was confused with rationalism (the pathological aspect of reason), and articulated in this lack of discernment, irrationalism was confused with freedom. Almost all 20th century art, with Surrealism at its head, made this mistake. The fallacy still exists despite the emergence of “irrationalism” and the consequent relativist currents that followed it having been consolidated everywhere. Essentially, much of what happened in the 20th century revolved around this clash between two paradigms: Rationalism versus Irationalism. Now, I see, we must save indeterminacy, chance, randomness from the terrible temptation of the cage of causalities. Holism, in its pathological form, may be reductionism that sets itself up as a new “eschatological narrative”. Is it “by chance” that holism emerges at a time when neo-liberalism and its cynical contempt for the human person is besieging everything, all freedoms, all acquired rights? Holistic awareness has brought a positive impact to greater ecological awareness, but followed with scholastic rigor, it stifles reality under the blanket of a determinism that is much more than uncomfortable: all great authoritarian ideologies are holistic. My friend will know how to combine her holistic and transpersonal belief with the freedom and responsibility that each moment asks of us — but how many will make the distinction, under a Law that explains and encompasses everything? How many will abandon themselves to what is? The awareness of the indeterminate brings tragic awareness, as the Greeks knew, but it also brings agency and the courage of decision. And we cannot give up on this. Furthermore, as Drummond shows in the poem “Quadrilha”, by Carlos Drummond de Andrade: «João loved Teresa who loved Raimundo/ who loved Maria who loved Joaquim who loved Lili/ who didn't love anyone./ João went to the United States, Teresa to the convent,/ Raimundo died of a disaster, Maria stayed with her aunt,/ Joaquim committed suicide and Lili married J. Pinto Fernandes/ who had not entered the story.» Real always runs outside the track, completely outside our concepts. That's what matters to us — a J. Pinto Fernandes emerges at the exact moment when we thought we had everything under control.

item.title: From Nobel and others
item.description: 9/10/22 When reading many people's reactions when this year's Nobel Prize for Literature was announced, I couldn't help but remember this comment from Jean-Pierre Siméon, in relation to another Nobel Prize: «An editor of a large national daily newspaper wrote five lines of indignation at the fact that the Nobel Prize for Literature had been awarded to Thomas Traströmer, a complete stranger. But unknown to whom? How did it not occur to this literary columnist, patented and paid to do his job, that his apparent ignorance was a matter of professional misconduct? Tranströmer had been translated into many languages, it was available in French.» There is always the chorus of those who wait for the Prize to be announced to complain about not having been invited to be on the jury, there is a subgroup of angry people who profess “I have never read it and I won’t even bother to read it”; It is a less than humble fauna, the one that inhabits social networks. I don't always know all the winners – I didn't know about the existence of Louise Glück, who I think is a great poet, or Olga Tokarczuk, for example – but this only brings me the comfort of knowing that the world is not a soap opera where everyone knows everyone else and the joy of recognizing that the yardstick of the world is not measured with my short measure and that, on the contrary, it gives me a new opportunity to face the unknown and learn from it. And perhaps the most stupid reaction was that of the intelligent man who wrote, But are there still people who read French?; simply confirming that today there is a new type of ignorance that is being raised with abandon: that of those who only read English. I would also prefer that, in French, Pascal Quignard, Linda Lê or even Sollers were awarded, or, among the new ones, Mathias Énard, but that does not detract from the dignity of Annie Ernaux's work (and it would have been enough to have written “Les Années”). There are books of hers that I like less, like the vulgar “Uma Paixão Simples”, but that happens with almost all authors, they have pivotal books and others that are like breaks in their itinerary. I recommend reading a beautiful book of interviews with her, “L’écriture comme un couteau”. 11/10/22 Yesterday an article appeared in the morning newspaper “Notícias”, in Maputo, reporting that the Pen Club Narrative Prize had been awarded to my wife, Teresa Noronha, for her soap opera “Tornado”, which had already won the Maria Velho da Costa Prize. The summary they made of the book does not even deserve comments, as it is pathetic, but what is important to highlight is the note of disqualification regarding its “Mozambicanness” when they call it Luso-Mozambican. It's not that it's inaccurate, but, for those who know the context, it's a touch naughty. Because finally, following the same logic, Adelino Timóteo should be called Tsonga-Mozambican, Álvaro Taruma Ronga-Mozambican, and Macua-Mozambican for writers from the north, etc., etc. Which doesn't appear to happen. At one point, in the novel, she writes: «Our color was never the dominant one. In colonial times we were not white, we were dressed in monhés, mugs of washed asses, the pejorative term to speak of a son of Goan and Portuguese. In the post-colonial period, I was not black and if, in Lisbon, they took me for Brazilian or Cape Verdean, in France they were lost in thought about where I was from and were surprised when they discovered that I was African. There I had to explain the wanderings of my DNA before settling in Mozambique. It took me a long time to accept that it was like that because yes, and that it was no curse that that was the case; on the contrary, I could even discover intense patterns of fantasy and freedom in this harlequin cloak that is our genealogy…” In modern-day Mozambique, it means being white… and that immediately takes precedence over what you do or what you are. It's sad, but as Naipaul warned, in these parts race is everything and people live as deeply immersed in it as, in other places, in religion. 12/10/22 Bruno Latour, an anthropologist who studied the sciences and their procedures and legitimations, law, technique and religions and who entertained himself by turning his back on the dogmas of the “moderns”, has died. For him, the place, the role of the specialist, was defined more by the perplexity he embodied than by the supposed truth that would sustain him. He proposed, as an alternative to the goodness of multiculturalism and its equivocal cliché “all different, all equal”, a return to “diplomacy”, given that this, precisely, does not start from the assumption that “we are unified because we share the same nature” but simply highlights that we “still do not share a common world”; It is necessary that we deal head-on with the “abyss of disagreement” that separates us, instead of trying to deceive it. And about politics he said, boldly: «If transparency is demanded from politicians, we kill them; if anyone demands fidelity to what the other was or what he says, kill him; If a mimetic relationship is required between what the crowd wants and what its representative says, representation is killed. These must therefore, by definition, betray, deceive, modify, distort the word. Without this ability to bend the word and betray, political language would not exist. I put forward the perfectly plausible hypothesis that political vocabulary will become as inaccessible as the religious language of the last twenty, three or fifty years.» (in “Un Monde Pluriel mais Commun”). I believe we will be needed to decipher this frantically complex world that has fallen to us.

item.title: Fuxi
item.description: O my friends, companions of these disastrous journeys through countries as old as the stars and the Moon, and as strange as the deepest interior of our wretched souls, what surprises still await us, what secret magics will we still have to overcome, what horrors will arise before our pupils dilated with astonishment and terror? Increasingly closer to our precarious existences, howls the monster of war, which threatens to merge humanity once and for all with the earth, incessantly traversed by the squalid specter of hunger and the invisible evil of ten thousand pestilences. Oh damned human, who believes you are superior to Nature itself, but then you reveal yourself, time and time again, incapable of simply ordering your existence and do not understand that you are possessed by fickle anger and are a slave to your own ambition! You are not satisfied with white jade or resplendent gold, there is no treasure that quenches your inextinguishable thirst for command over other men, animals and things; for eternal anguish dwells in you; in you the expelled serpents crawl; for you grows, powerful and impressive, the most fertile ivy of Evil! This is perhaps why on the Mount they call the White Deer, where one of the eight immortals on a sacred mount soared through the heavens towards Penglai, lives the fuxi, a bird whose shape resembles that of a rooster, although topped by a human head. What strange copulations, what monstrous embraces, what terrible procedures must have occurred to make the existence of this sinister animal possible? We don't know, but all it takes is for our exhausted mind to dare to come up with an answer, and then we feel our skin covered in horrendous goosebumps and our heart races in parade, like a wild horse whipped by the night whip of fear, unresponsive to reins and containment. Let men refrain from visiting Monte do Veado, because although there are riches capable of calming the wildest ambitions, if they have the unfortunate luck of catching a glimpse of a fuxi, it is certain that a war will soon break out and the cultivated fields will perish, because of it the tame animals will be beheaded, because of it the best young men of two generations will be sacrificed and the pride of the countries will be destroyed. The fuxi makes a sound reminiscent of its name. If you hear it, dark night or clear day, be on your way, because the path you are on is the surest path to misfortune!

item.title: Procrastinators Club
item.description: While rereading Easter, because of an article I'm preparing, I come across this excerpt, taken from “The Universal Man”: «Each man, morally, is one and absolute; but he has to live with attenuating his personality. (…) Coexistence is only possible between people reduced to a negative or hollow presence, which constantly gives way. Hence, the emptiness of society, an immense ghost made up of countless dead people. A superior man is antisocial or criminal. His destiny is prison and the desert”. I hesitate to think whether Pascoaes reflects here on the political-social conditions that muzzle, and, worse, in a sad country like Portugal in the middle of the Estado Novo (and the book is from 1931) where any possibilities of spontaneity and frankness in human relationships were restricted, or if it merely lends to the behavior of (oppressed) men a metaphysical framework, an “essence”, declared in the presumption of each being, in isolation, “one and absolute”. Sometimes, in addition to an excess of Spirituality (or Saudade), Pascoaes lacks the world. He is a great poet, but he is a poet who takes Ampoules-of-Great-Ideas and who therefore writes often trapped by a programmatic furor, ceasing to have pores; It is not uncommon for a glass to be erected between him and reality, the same glass that made him write in O Bailado, from 1921: «Everything is a ghost. There are only clouds, clouds of voices, clouds of souls, of afflictions and tragedies! Clouds and more clouds, appearances and more appearances! And a divine lightning that pierces them, the instantaneous Apparition that appears and throws us to the ground, dazzled!» Sometimes I felt like reminding him, as António Sérgio did, in the article “Regeneration and Tradition, Moral and Economy”, which he dedicated to Pascoaes: «Pascoais, Pascoais my dear friend: you are a pure, excellent and noble poet, but also a victim of this social environment, like us all: of this horrible isolation that you praise and I curse». «As a child, he stole two blackbirds», says Jacinto do Prado Coelho, he stole them from a nest, and he felt remorse for that all his life. The Person, few more mischiefs are known to him. Only in Álvaro de Campos does a hint of wickedness and obscenity emerge: «Ah, and the ordinary and dirty people, who always look the same,/ Who use swear words as usual words,/ Whose sons steal from grocery store doors/ And whose daughters at the age of eight – and I think this is beautiful and I love it! –/ Decent-looking men masturbate in stairwells.” (Triumphal ode). Both poets, driven by a certain idea of ​​sanctity, had the same inability to break the siege of their skin with the overflow of someone else's dam, the same curse of sublimating the concrete ferocity of eros with the gentleness of the verses. Obviously, they are both “bigger than life”, but sometimes it makes me unnerved to watch, from my position as a hatter at the Procrastinators Club, the tantrums between them, just to see who occupies, in the internal organization, the place of President and Treasurer. Although Pessoa guessed more things, for example, what he wrote in the lesser-known Ode Martial and which is reflected in the situation we live in today, in a different way, with his heart in his hands: «MARTIAL ODE (…) Helahoho! helahoho! The poor widow's sewing machine who was bayoneted... She sewed indefinitely in the afternoon… The table where the old people played, Everything mixed, everything mixed with bodies, with blood, All one river, one wave, one dragging horror. Helahoho! helahoho! I unearthed the child's tin train pressed into the middle of the [road, And I cried like all the mothers in the world about the horror of life. My pantheistic feet tripped over the sewing machine [widow who was killed with a bayonet And that poor instrument of peace stuck a spear in my heart. Yes, I was the one to blame for everything, I was the soldier for them all Who killed, raped, burned and broke, It was me and my shame and my remorse like a shadow [deformed They travel around the world like Ashavero, But behind my steps there are footsteps the size of infinity And a physical fear of being accountable to God makes me close my eyes. [eyes suddenly. (…) I ordered, captain, to shoot the trembling peasants, I let the daughters of all fathers tied to trees be raped, Now I saw that it was inside my heart that all this happened, And everything scalds and suffocates and I can't move without it [everything be the same. God have mercy on me, I didn't have it from anyone! LÀ-BAS, JE NE SAIS OÙ…” What is clear today, at this disastrous moment in which Cristiano & Companhia failed in their mission in Braga, and in which the self-sabotage of Russia's gas pipelines reminds us that Putin will indeed be willing to do anything to maintain dominance over the areas endorsed, is that, bomb for bomb, the “bomb” of the Fifth Empire was preferable. However, even in this, my dears, we were irresolute, a little more on the side of piela than determined, and we procrastinated.

item.title: Middle East | China says it is carrying out “active mediation”
item.description: The Chinese Government stated yesterday that it is developing “active mediation” in the conflict in the Middle East and that it will continue to maintain contacts with the parties involved to promote the return to negotiations. Foreign Ministry spokesman Guo Jiakun said at a press conference that China is “deeply concerned” about tensions and called on all parties to cease military operations to avoid further escalation of the conflict. Guo recalled that the head of Chinese diplomacy, Wang Yi, had recent contacts with several counterparts in the region to “exchange in-depth opinions on the regional situation”. The spokesperson added that the Chinese Government's special envoy for the Middle East, Zhai Jun, is currently in the region carrying out visits as part of Beijing's mediation efforts, and indicated that the Chinese authorities will inform “in due course” about their agenda, without offering further details about the mission's objectives or itinerary. The Chinese Foreign Ministry only reported that Zhai arrived in Saudi Arabia on Sunday, where he met with Foreign Minister Faisal bin Farhan. According to Guo, the handling of the Iranian issue and other issues related to the Middle East must be based on principles such as “respecting national sovereignty”, “refraining from abuse of force” and “maintaining non-interference in internal affairs”. The spokesperson added that China will continue to maintain communication with the parties involved and strengthen mediation efforts to “promote a de-escalation of the situation”. Energy security The official added that China will adopt the “necessary measures” to safeguard its energy security and called for “stable and fluid” energy supplies to be guaranteed. Guo Jiakun said that energy security is “crucial for the world economy” and stated that all countries have a responsibility to ensure supply stability. The repercussions of the conflict in the Strait of Hormuz directly affect China, which receives around 45 percent of the oil it imports from Iran and several other countries. Guo added that Beijing will take measures to protect its energy security, although he did not specify the concrete policies the country will implement. The spokesperson also highlighted that the Strait of Hormuz and the surrounding waters are “important channels for international trade in goods and energy”, adding that maintaining security and stability in that area “responds to the common interest of the international community”. China, Tehran's main trading partner and its biggest oil buyer, has repeatedly condemned attacks on Iran by the United States and Israel for “violating the sovereignty” of the Persian country.

item.title: Japanese company fails to launch the country's first private satellite
item.description: Space One failed yesterday for the third consecutive time in its attempt to become the first Japanese company to put satellites into orbit. The Kairos III rocket managed to take off at 11:10 local time, as scheduled, from Wakayama prefecture, in western Japan, but was forced to abort the launch mission shortly after takeoff. Just five minutes after takeoff, the Japanese company announced the decision to abort the mission, without providing further information for now, while it continues to “investigate the details” of what happened, according to a statement from Space One. The launch of the rocket, 18 meters high, had already been postponed three times, the last time on Wednesday, due to a failure in connections with the satellites it was carrying. The objective of the mission was to place five satellites into orbit at around 500 kilometers, according to Japanese public broadcaster NHK. If it had succeeded, the company would have become the first in the country to place a satellite in orbit, a milestone that, for now, remains to be achieved. History of failures Space One launched the first Kairos rocket in March 2024, but the device exploded about five minutes after takeoff due to an error in predicting the thrust. In December of the same year, it launched the second device, Kairos II, which self-destructed after those responsible for the project concluded that it would be difficult to carry out the mission due to problems with the sensors. Founded in 2018, the company seeks to commercialize low-cost space transportation services and offer regular rocket launches exclusively with private funds. The failure represents a new setback for the Japanese space industry's efforts and ambitions to compete on a global level with companies such as North American SpaceX.

item.title: Defense | Budget to rise 7 percent
item.description: China announced yesterday a 7 percent increase in the Defense budget for 2026, maintaining the trend of recent years, in an effort to reinforce deterrence against the United States and its position in regional disputes. The amount was announced at the opening of the annual session of the National People's Assembly, the Chinese legislative body, which takes place in Beijing. According to published data, China plans to spend 1.9096 billion yuan (around 238 billion euros) on defense this year, an amount that remains around three times lower than the United States' military budget. The increase remains in line with increases recorded over the last decade, which have generally ranged between 7 percent and 8 percent per year. In 2025, the Chinese military budget increased by 7.2 percent. Analysts indicate that the new resources should finance salary increases for military personnel, exercises and training, military maneuvers near Taiwan, the strengthening of cyber defense capabilities and the acquisition of more advanced military equipment. According to Song Zhongping, a military commentator and former Chinese Army instructor, China intends to maintain an independent foreign policy, which requires strengthening military and technological capabilities. "The country wants an independent foreign policy. Our military and technological capabilities must accompany this ambition, otherwise this policy will be subject to coercion or even domination by other countries, particularly the United States", he stated. Despite the increase, Chinese military expenditure remains relatively moderate when compared as a percentage of gross domestic product (GDP). China currently has only one military base abroad, located in Djibouti, and claims that its defense policy aims exclusively to protect its territorial sovereignty, including Taiwan, which it considers part of its territory.

item.title: APN | “One China” principle reaffirmed
item.description: China yesterday reaffirmed its commitment to the “one China” principle and guaranteed that it will combat separatist forces that promote “Taiwan independence”, said Chinese Premier Li Qiang. The statements were made during the presentation of the Government's annual work report at the session of the National People's Congress (NPC), the Chinese legislative body, which opened yesterday at the Grand Palace of the People, in Beijing. Li stressed that Beijing will continue to advance “national reunification” and “thoroughly implement the [Communist] Party’s overall strategy to resolve the Taiwan issue in the new era.” According to the report presented to the ANP, the Chinese Government will act in accordance with the “one China” principle and the so-called 1992 Consensus, while at the same time opposing what it classified as “interference by external forces”. The Chief Executive added that Beijing intends to promote the “peaceful development of cross-strait relations” and encourage exchanges, cooperation and integration between Taiwan and the mainland. The Taiwan issue continues to be one of the main sources of tension in the region. No mês passado, Pequim acusou o líder taiwanês de ser um “instigador de guerra”, após William Lai Ching-te ter alertado para o impacto regional que poderia resultar de um eventual controlo chinês da ilha. Analistas taiwaneses citados por órgãos de comunicação locais consideram, contudo, pouco provável uma acção militar chinesa a curto prazo, tendo em conta o actual contexto internacional, marcado também pelas tensões relacionadas com o conflito no Irão. According to these experts, Beijing continues to maintain the so-called “peaceful reunification” as its official objective.

item.title: Economy | Growth target set between 4.5% and 5% for 2026
item.description: China yesterday set an economic growth target of between 4.5 percent and 5 percent for this year, slightly below the objectives set in recent years, in a context marked by the prolonged crisis in the real estate sector and external uncertainties. The goal was announced by Chinese Premier Li Qiang during the presentation of the Government's work report at the opening session of the National People's Congress (ANP), China's highest legislative body. The report establishes the objective of growth in this range, adding that the Government will seek to “achieve better results in practice”. “While we recognize our achievements, we are also fully aware of the difficulties and challenges we face,” the report states. Over the past three years, Beijing has set growth targets of “around 5 percent.” In 2025, the Chinese economy expanded by 5 percent. By establishing a range between 4.5 percent and 5 percent, the Government seeks to provide greater room for maneuver to adjust economic policies throughout the year. The document highlights the increase in geopolitical risks and points out that free trade is under serious threat. Chinese exports to the United States were affected by tariffs imposed by US President Donald Trump, although China has expanded sales to other regions of the world. Domestically, the report highlights a “marked” imbalance between strong supply and weak demand, as well as the challenge of transitioning the economy to new growth drivers. “Internally, we still face several problems and challenges, both old and new”, adds the document. “When proposing these goals, we took into account the need to leave some space for structural adjustments, risk prevention and reforms in the first year of this five-year plan period, in order to establish a solid foundation to achieve better results in the coming years”, states the report. Other plans The ANP's annual session, which brings together around 3,000 delegates and is considered the country's main annual political event, is also expected to approve a five-year plan that will define China's political and economic priorities until 2030. The document also includes commitments to strengthen the domestic economy and, at the same time, advance Chinese President Xi Jinping's ambitions to transform the country into a global leader in technology.

item.title: CPPCC | Open main annual political meeting
item.description:
China yesterday kicked off its main annual political meeting with the opening of the session of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), the consultative body that precedes the inauguration, on Thursday, of the National People's Assembly, a legislative body. Chinese President Xi Jinping participated in the inaugural session held at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, accompanied by other members of the leadership of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). During the meeting, the work on the agenda was approved and Wang Huning, considered one of the regime's main ideologues and close to Xi, presented the activity report of the consultative body's standing committee. The opening of the so-called “Two Sessions”, China's biggest annual political event, takes place at the beginning of the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030), which will define the country's economic and strategic priorities for the next five years. Tomorrow, Prime Minister Li Qiang is expected to announce the economic growth target for 2026, which, according to analysts, could be between 4.5 percent and 5 percent. The annual meeting also takes place after the expulsion, in recent weeks, of several senior military officials from both the National Popular Assembly and the consultative body itself, as part of the anti-corruption campaign that has reduced the leadership of the armed forces. The Defense budget, which in last year's session increased by 7.2 percent year-on-year, and political messages aimed at the Armed Forces should also focus part of the attention during the debates.
Global tensions The session is also taking place in a context of growing international tensions, following attacks by the United States and Israel against Iran, and before a planned visit by US President Donald Trump to China, which has not yet been officially confirmed by Beijing. In the first contacts with the local press, some members of the consultative body highlighted topics such as industrial and technological modernization. The president of car manufacturer Geely, Li Shufu, called, in statements cited by The Paper newspaper, to avoid “senseless competition” in the electric vehicle sector, while other delegates highlighted advances in quantum technology and the need to accelerate the regulation of autonomous vehicles and humanoid robotics. During the session of the legislative body, Foreign Minister Wang Yi is expected to hold his usual annual press conference, an event often used to anticipate the tone of Chinese diplomacy in an increasingly volatile international context.

item.title: Teaching | China prepares law that imposes Mandarin as the main language
item.description: China is preparing to pass a law that will make Mandarin the main language of instruction for ethnic minorities. According to the British newspaper Financial Times, the draft Law for the Promotion of Ethnic Unity and Progress is expected to be approved this week during the annual meeting of the Chinese parliament, the National People's Congress. The diploma establishes that Mandarin will become the main language of instruction in schools and universities attended by minorities, allowing languages ​​such as Tibetan, Uighur or Mongolian to be taught only as a second language, no longer being able to be used in core subjects. The text also provides for legal action against individuals or organizations, inside or outside China, that “undermine national unity” or incite “separatism”, and states that ethnic unity “should not be interfered with by external forces under the pretext of ethnicity, religion or human rights”. Analysts cited by the newspaper consider that the new law represents a structural change in the State's approach to minorities. For critics, the legislation is part of a broader effort at cultural assimilation that has been intensifying under the leadership of Xi Jinping, in a country where the Han majority represents around 90 percent of the population of 1.4 billion inhabitants, but which officially recognizes 56 ethnic groups and several languages ​​with their own writing systems. Neil Thomas, a researcher at the Asia Society's China Analysis Center, said the law "will expand the legal basis for restricting religious, cultural and political activities among minority groups." Wang Yanzhong, a researcher linked to the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, argued that the diploma aims to provide legal safeguards for the Communist Party's work on ethnic matters and contribute to “maintaining the security and stability of border regions and ethnic areas”. The academic also argued that Mandarin offers greater educational and professional advantages, arguing that the number of speakers of minority languages ​​is decreasing among younger generations.

item.title: China / USA | Reinforcement of investment before Trump’s arrival
item.description: China and the United States are discussing ways to reactivate reciprocal investment as part of preparations for the US President's planned visit to Beijing, the Hong Kong newspaper South China Morning Post reported yesterday. Citing anonymous sources, the newspaper said that the signing of agreements in the area of ​​investment could be one of the few tangible results of the trip, announced by Washington for March 31 to April 2, but not yet officially confirmed by Beijing. Uncertainty surrounding the visit and possible results has increased in recent days, following attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran, a country of which China is the main trading partner and largest buyer of oil, as well as one of its most important diplomatic supports. According to the newspaper, although there are no specific agreements yet, both Beijing and Washington have expressed interest in structures such as joint companies with clear governance models, licensing agreements or formats with less exposure in terms of intellectual property, capable of resisting political and regulatory scrutiny. One of the sources cited points to the agreement reached in 2023 between Ford and the Chinese CATL, the world's largest manufacturer of batteries for electric vehicles, as a “possible model”, which allowed the North American company to license lithium iron phosphate battery technology for use in the United States. Entre os pontos de fricção, os representantes norte-americanos terão insistido na necessidade de melhorar o acesso ao mercado chinês, onde empresas estrangeiras denunciam há anos tratamento desigual. Pequim pediu maior protecção para os seus investimentos face a perdas e retiradas motivadas por maior escrutínio e por tarifas, abordando também as ofertas públicas iniciais de empresas chinesas nos mercados norte-americanos. The United States has traditionally been one of the main destinations for Chinese foreign investment, which reached a maximum of US$17 billion in 2016, falling to around US$6.6 billion in 2024. In total, China invested more than US$90 billion in the world's largest economy. According to data released by Beijing, US investment in China fell 18.5 percent in 2024, to around US$2.7 billion. According to one of the sources, it was conveyed that Chinese investment is welcome, as long as it is in sectors considered non-sensitive. However, for this to happen, it would be necessary to “change the narrative” and mitigate the discourse of the “Chinese threat” dominant in the United States. New round Last week, the Chinese Ministry of Commerce announced the holding of a sixth round of trade negotiations with Washington. Although the date and location have not been confirmed, the talks are expected to serve as a precursor to Trump's visit. The South China Morning Post also said that the trip could allow the extension of the one-year trade truce agreed in October between Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, which helped to temporarily ease tensions generated by the tariff escalation that began after the Republican's return to the White House.

item.title: Tehran | China accuses the United States and Israel of “inciting regime change”
item.description: China yesterday accused the United States and Israel of “inciting regime change” in Iran, following the attacks launched on February 28, classifying the assassination of the leader of the Islamic republic, Ali Khamenei, as “unacceptable”. Chinese Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Mao Ning reiterated at a press conference that the US and Israeli offensive “violates international law and the basic norms that govern international relations”. The statements come after telephone conversations held the day before by the head of Chinese diplomacy, Wang Yi, with his counterparts from Iran, Oman and France. Mao structured the Chinese position on three axes: immediate cessation of military operations, return to dialogue and opposition to unilateral actions. The official stressed that Beijing supports Iran in defending its sovereignty, while taking into account the “legitimate security concerns” of the Gulf countries. Regarding the nuclear issue, the spokesperson stated that China respects “Iran's legitimate right to the peaceful use of nuclear energy” and argued that the issue must return “to the path of political and diplomatic resolution”. China, Iran's main trading partner and largest importer of its oil, had already condemned on Sunday the death of Ali Khamenei during the offensive led by Israel and the United States. Since Saturday, Israel, in coordination with Washington, has been bombing several positions in Iran, claiming to seek to destroy arsenals and ballistic missile production capabilities and put an end to the ayatollah regime. Iran | Beijing calls for the preservation of security in the Strait of Hormuz China yesterday appealed to all parties involved in the war in the Middle East to guarantee the safety of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, warning of the risk of impact on the world economy. “China urges all parties to immediately cease military operations, avoid further escalation of tensions, maintain the security of shipping routes in the Strait of Hormuz and prevent the situation from having a broader impact on the global economy,” said Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Mao Ning, at a regular press conference. The Strait of Hormuz, which connects the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman, is one of the world's main energy arteries, through which around a fifth of the oil traded globally by sea passes through. At the beginning of 2025, China was the main destination for oil that crosses that strategic route. In recent years, the country was also the largest importer of Iranian crude, in a context of closer economic relations between Beijing and Tehran. The statements come at a time of strong regional instability, following the attacks launched on February 28 by the United States and Israel against Iran, which responded with missiles and unmanned aerial vehicles ('drones') against North American bases and targets in Gulf countries. The increased risk in the region led several shipping companies to suspend or divert routes in the Strait of Hormuz, fueling fears of disruptions in energy supplies and a further rise in crude oil prices. Beijing has reiterated the need for an immediate ceasefire and return to dialogue, while underlining the importance of preserving the stability of global supply chains.

item.title: Miguel Carvalho, author of the book “Por Dentro do Chega”: “Chega attracted everything and a pair of boots”
item.description:
Journalist Miguel Carvalho has long been investigating Chega, the far-right political party founded by André Ventura. Guest of the “Rota das Letras” Literary Festival, Miguel Carvalho comes to Macau to talk about the book “Por Dentro do Chega – A Face Oculta da Extrema-Rireita em Portugal” and the growth of populism He will share the session “From Mistake to Populism” with another journalist, João Miguel Tavares, who wrote a work about José Sócrates. What parallels do you find between Sócrates and Chega? I find it quite curious that the debate between these two books [“José Sócrates – Ascensão (1957-2005)” and “Por Dentro do Chega”] took place. This is because André Ventura himself assumed, although he later denied it, but it is recorded, that he voted for José Sócrates in the first term. I describe in the book the exemplary way in which Ventura, in some way, looks at himself in relation to his own political persona, in the positive characteristics he saw in José Sócrates, in relation to the idea of ​​the politician who came to change everything and anything. But José Sócrates is a hated figure within Chega, he is, perhaps, the politician that Chega refers to most to identify the bad guys in politics. In many cases, Sócrates was the motivation why many joined Chega. In my research I found many activists who voted for José Sócrates, believing it to be the best thing that could happen to Portuguese politics. The book is in its ninth edition in Portugal. Did you expect this reaction from the public, taking into account the growth of Chega's electorate? There is a dimension to this that, for me, is a little unreal. We are talking about a country like Portugal, which has marginal reading rates, and we already have 36 thousand copies in circulation in total. But this reaction from Chega's electoral universe, both from people who have already left or who are still there, is not a complete surprise to me. I received a lot of messages via email or Facebook from people who had either left Chega or were still there, but who respected my journalistic work, regardless of what they might think of me as a citizen or my convictions. People who were reading the book with a critical mind. I know people who read the book and left Chega, and others who continued. The book is 90 percent Talking About Itself, and I'm grateful that people continue to respect my journalistic work, regardless of their opinion of me.
Chega is the result of a wave of populism that has emerged all over the world. Have we learned nothing from history? Not that, and of course at different rates. I dedicate the last chapter to the entry of extremist narratives into schools and colleges, and how the Chega narrative is contaminating, and making complicit, a generation that has a great distance from the 25th of April. Based on testimonies from teachers and other professionals, I report the increasingly frequent presence of students in classrooms giving the Nazi salute, some who quoted parts of “Mein Kampf”, or adopted simplistic narratives, most of them completely false and based on the misinformation that is widespread. These are young people who are bringing things to the classroom that we thought were already resolved. But this also happens because something failed at school, and perhaps Portugal is not unique in this regard. Perhaps, in part, it justifies the rise of these narratives and movements. I also know, from access to Chega's vast internal documentation over the years, that teachers are one of the professional classes with the greatest representation in Chega's activism, from basic education to academia. Here we have factors that are generating a very worrying situation in relation to the entry of extremist ideas into a universe that, in many cases, still does not even vote. One of the ideas that comes out of your book is that we cannot talk about a Chega-type voter. It's not easy for me [to make this definition]. I don't know, at the moment, who the typical Chega voter is because it varies greatly depending on geographic location and political orientations. In other words, Chega attracted everything and a pair of boots, so to speak. Initially, he sought people from the parties that were closest to him ideologically, namely the PSD and the CDS. I went a lot into the field, interviewed hundreds of them, and it is now verifiable with studies carried out on electoral behavior that Chega managed to attract people from various ideological options in the past, from the PCP to the Left Bloc. There were many traditional Socialist Party voters who switched to Chega. The party is disruptive even in relation to the traditional party system itself. In what sense? I believe that a voter or a member of one of the traditional parties may even disagree with the leader at a certain point or disagree with the program at a certain stage, but if he is in that party and votes for that party, it is because he is minimally comfortable, let's say, 70 or 80 percent, with its ideas. Now, Chega doesn't work like that, and has come to break with all of this. We have to imagine Chega as a kind of restaurant where there is chef Ventura who invents about ten dishes, with those ideas that we know by heart and stir-fry, about immigration, the gypsy community, the chemical castration of pedophiles, and then this electoral universe chooses three or four dishes that are most relevant to them, depending on their personal and professional journey, or their frustrations, or their community, even. For example, it's easy to find people from Chega who don't even agree with André Ventura's speech about the gypsy community, but they agree when he justifies the need for there to be no sexual education in schools, because that would only serve to teach children how to masturbate. Then they already believe and think it is a fight worth fighting. Each case is different and I have never encountered a party so atomized and individualized at that level. A few years ago, for Visão magazine, he carried out an investigation into Macanese Vitório Cardoso, linked to the PSD, but seen at Chega demonstrations. Is this an example of someone who approached Chega from a traditional party? Everything I know about him and his public statements, some of which, however, have been deleted, and everything I have read over the years about the public expression of his political thought, makes him obviously a figure closer to Chega than to the PSD, even though this PSD is what it is, without much ideological support. It wouldn't surprise me, therefore, if people like Vitório felt much more comfortable in a party like Chega than in the one where they supposedly join. In the Portuguese diaspora, Chega has also grown. How do you explain this phenomenon, taking into account that we are talking about a party with an anti-immigration discourse? This side is less covered in the book, because at the time there were no conditions to delve deeper into the topic. Chega has, from the outset, seen a great mobilization on the part of some Portuguese businesspeople in the diaspora. In France and Switzerland this is very evident. There are, therefore, big businessmen who put themselves at the service of the party and André Ventura, using their resources to get as many people as possible to vote. Chega denied it, saying that it has nothing to do with these organizations, and that they are individual initiatives, but the truth is that even in the legislative elections in Geneva, Chega was the only party that managed to put electoral propaganda in the mailboxes. This reflects another aspect, which is the fact that the PS and PSD have been comfortable, in some countries, with the immigrant vote for too long. In other words, as they usually divided voters among themselves, they did not worry much about the political and media literacy of a vast immigration sector. It is necessary to remember that 90 percent of Portuguese people living in the most important countries for Portuguese immigration did not go to vote, and this is often justified by the fact that they feel that Portugal has forgotten them. And then there is another issue, which is the fact that even though people emigrated in very difficult circumstances to escape poverty, the truth is that with their work they achieved a certain status and today consider that any immigration, even from their Portuguese brothers, is harmful. I noticed this a lot in the Azores, for example, where family and friendship conflicts begin to generate. This, from a psychological point of view, is quite complex to dismantle. Representative José Pereira Coutinho, who won the last legislative elections in Macau, compared himself to André Ventura. Is the president of Chega seen as a kind of political star who almost serves as a reference for others? Yes. It is already clear that the party would have many difficulties in maintaining the political strength it has today without André Ventura, who is someone who has dominated media language for many years, long before Chega. He has an overwhelming command of communication and knows how to simplify, obviously behind many lies and falsehoods. I have been writing this since 2020, Chega is, essentially, a project of personal power. Ventura subjected Chega to the leader's cult, and the party is today, without a doubt, less free than it was at the beginning. With less internal criticism? There were, despite everything, certain currents and people with their own thoughts, who, whether or not they agreed with most of the proposals that the party offered, were capable of taking the stage at a congress and criticizing the leadership. I have serious doubts that this will happen at the next Chega congress. The rulings of the Constitutional Court are overwhelming regarding the lack of internal democracy, and [this happens] out of survival instinct, as André Ventura rarely allowed to give [the stage] to other people. There are recent studies that demonstrate that André Ventura transformed Chega into a leadership very similar to that of the North Korean regime. You can ask me, if he leaves tomorrow, obviously Chega will remain. It suffers a great shock, but we still have the big question, just as we had in the United States: it was not because Donald Trump was out of the White House for four years that he ceased to exist. Here we have the same problem, it will not be because Ventura leaves that “Ventura Country” will cease to exist, it is necessary to respond to the desires and frustrations that generated this “Ventura Country”. If this is not done, this “Ventura Country” will go looking for another Ventura. Will Ventura be prime minister in Portugal in the next legislative period? With or without an absolute majority? If there is a political crisis again, I am convinced that Chega has a high chance of becoming a Government. But the truth is that there are signs of wear and tear in Chega’s narrative. From the electorate that joined in strongly from the beginning, there are signs that it found other types of motivations. Between the local elections and the first round of the presidential elections, and this was not talked about much, Chega lost around 100 thousand votes. These are very different elections, but 100,000 votes are not lost like that either. And the truth is that Chega had, in relation to the last local councils, very high expectations which, even with three municipal councils won, were not confirmed. The truth is that, if there is no immediate change in the political cycle, Chega will have more difficulty reaching the Government.

item.title: Tracy Choi’s new film “Girlfriends” premieres today at MGM Macau: “It’s very difficult to be a director in Macau”
item.description: In “Girlfriends”, her new film and fourth feature film of her career, Tracy Choi created a kind of alter-ego, through a character who, after studying in Taiwan, lives in Hong Kong while struggling to finance a film. In an interview with HM, the director says she wants to continue making films about women and their experiences. The official premiere of “Girlfriends” is scheduled for today, at 7:30 pm, at MGM Macau It's been exactly ten years since “Sisterhood” premiered, and Tracy Choi probably wouldn't have imagined that, a decade later, the success would be real, with more and more international recognition for her way of making films. After placing a Macau film at the Busan International Film Festival for the first time, with “Girlfriends”, it is the local director’s turn to bring her new film, the fourth feature film of her career, to your home. The premiere takes place today at the “Treasure Box” room at MGM Macau, starting at 7:30 pm. We spoke to the director before the premiere of the new work, who told us how she started thinking about the film's script during the Covid-19 period. "The story is about a girl who grew up in Macau, who studied in Taiwan and went to work in Hong Kong. I thought about it [the film] during the pandemic, because we couldn't go anywhere and we had nothing to do. I decided to write a script about myself: a girl from Macau growing up, seeing how she grows up and how she finds herself, something like that." The cast is made up of actresses that Tracy Choi has worked with, namely Fish Liew, Jennifer Yu, Elizabeth Tang, Ning Han, Natalie Hsu and Eliz Lao.
